I've uploaded a code repository in the directory /testbed. Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
`createDiffHTML` API fails with "Not a changeset: undefined"
**Description**

I'm encountering a 500 Internal Server Error when calling the `createDiffHTML` API endpoint. Regardless of the startRev and endRev specified, the function fails with the error:

```
[ERROR] API - Error: Not a changeset: undefined
  at PadDiff._createDiffAtext (/run/etherpad-lite/src/node/utils/padDiff.ts:182:15)
  at applyToAText (/run/etherpad-lite/src/static/js/Changeset.ts:1061:9)
  at applyToText (/run/etherpad-lite/src/static/js/Changeset.ts:405:20)
  at async <anonymous> (/run/etherpad-lite/src/node/hooks/express/openapi.ts:679:22)
  at async Object.exports.createDiffHTML (/run/etherpad-lite/src/node/db/API.ts:855:16)
  at async OpenAPIBackend.handleRequest (/run/etherpad-lite/node_modules/.pnpm/openapi-backend@5.11.1/node_modules/openapi-backend/src/7)
  at async PadDiff.getHtml (/run/etherpad-lite/src/node/utils/padDiff.ts:194:19)
  at async handler (/run/etherpad-lite/src/node/hooks/express/openapi.ts:633:20)
  at error (/run/etherpad-lite/src/static/js/Changeset.ts:64:13)
  at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
  at unpack (/run/etherpad-lite/src/static/js/Changeset.ts:363:44)
```

Notably, this even happens if the startRev and endRev are the same. 

This suggests that Etherpad is failing to retrieve or process an expected changeset, even though individual revision changesets are retrievable.

**Steps to Reproduce**

1. Create a new pad.
2. Make a few edits to create multiple revisions.
3. Call the API with:
`GET /api/1.3.0/createDiffHTML?padID=<padID>&startRev=1&endRev=1&apikey=<API_KEY>`
4. The API returns 500 Internal Server Error with `"Not a changeset: undefined"`.
5. Additionally, `createDiffHTML(1,2)` and `createDiffHTML(1,10)` fail, while individual revision changesets (`getRevisionChangeset`) return correctly.

**Expected Behavior**

`createDiffHTML(1,1)` should return valid diff HTML (even if it's an empty or minimal diff).
`createDiffHTML(1,2)` should successfully compute a diff between the first two revisions.

**Actual Behavior**

The API call fails with `"Not a changeset: undefined"`.
Etherpad logs show an error originating from `Changeset.ts`, indicating it is unable to apply or retrieve an expected changeset.

**Server (please complete the following information):**
-  
 - Etherpad version: `2.2.7`
 - API Version: `1.3.0`
 - OS: `Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS`
 - Node.js version (`node --version`): `v20.18.0`
 - npm version (`npm --version`): `10.8.2`
 - Is the server free of plugins: No. 

**Troubleshooting Done**

✅ Verified individual revision changesets (`getRevisionChangeset`) are retrievable.
✅ Tested smaller revision ranges (`createDiffHTML(1,5)`, `createDiffHTML(1,1)`) — still fails.
✅ Tested on brand new pads — still fails.
✅ Checked the Etherpad GitHub issues (no existing issue found).

**Potential Causes**

- Bug in createDiffHTML Logic?
- Changeset Processing Bug: Etherpad might fail to apply early changesets correctly.
- Revision 0 Issue: The initial changeset might not be properly interpreted when computing diffs.


</pr_description>

     2	
     3	import {PadAuthor, PadType} from "../types/PadType";
     4	import {MapArrayType} from "../types/MapType";
     5	
     6	import AttributeMap from '../../static/js/AttributeMap';
     7	import {applyToAText, checkRep, compose, deserializeOps, pack, splitAttributionLines, splitTextLines, unpack} from '../../static/js/Changeset';
     8	import {Builder} from "../../static/js/Builder";
     9	import {OpAssembler} from "../../static/js/OpAssembler";
    10	import {numToString} from "../../static/js/ChangesetUtils";
    11	import Op from "../../static/js/Op";
    12	import {StringAssembler} from "../../static/js/StringAssembler";
    13	const attributes = require('../../static/js/attributes');
    14	const exportHtml = require('./ExportHtml');
    15	
    16	
    17	class PadDiff {
    18	  private readonly _pad: PadType;
    19	    private readonly _fromRev: string;
    20	    private readonly _toRev: string;
    21	    private _html: any;
    22	    public _authors: any[];
    23	    private self: PadDiff | undefined
    24	  constructor(pad: PadType, fromRev:string, toRev:string) {
    25	    // check parameters
    26	    if (!pad || !pad.id || !pad.atext || !pad.pool) {
    27	      throw new Error('Invalid pad');
    28	    }
    29	
    30	    const range = pad.getValidRevisionRange(fromRev, toRev);
    31	    if (!range) throw new Error(`Invalid revision range. startRev: ${fromRev} endRev: ${toRev}`);
    32	
    33	    this._pad = pad;
    34	    this._fromRev = range.startRev;
    35	    this._toRev = range.endRev;
    36	    this._html = null;
    37	    this._authors = [];
    38	  }
    39	  _isClearAuthorship(changeset: any){
    40	    // unpack
    41	    const unpacked = unpack(changeset);
    42	
    43	    // check if there is nothing in the charBank
    44	    if (unpacked.charBank !== '') {
    45	      return false;
    46	    }
    47	
    48	    // check if oldLength == newLength
    49	    if (unpacked.oldLen !== unpacked.newLen) {
    50	      return false;
    51	    }
    52	
    53	    const [clearOperator, anotherOp] = deserializeOps(unpacked.ops);
    54	
    55	    // check if there is only one operator
    56	    if (anotherOp != null) return false;
    57	
    58	    // check if this operator doesn't change text
    59	    if (clearOperator.opcode !== '=') {
    60	      return false;
    61	    }
    62	
    63	    // check that this operator applys to the complete text
    64	    // if the text ends with a new line, its exactly one character less, else it has the same length
    65	    if (clearOperator.chars !== unpacked.oldLen - 1 && clearOperator.chars !== unpacked.oldLen) {
    66	      return false;
    67	    }
    68	
    69	    const [appliedAttribute, anotherAttribute] =
    70	        attributes.attribsFromString(clearOperator.attribs, this._pad.pool);
    71	
    72	    // Check that the operation has exactly one attribute.
    73	    if (appliedAttribute == null || anotherAttribute != null) return false;
    74	
    75	    // check if the applied attribute is an anonymous author attribute
    76	    if (appliedAttribute[0] !== 'author' || appliedAttribute[1] !== '') {
    77	      return false;
    78	    }
    79	
    80	    return true;
    81	  }
    82	  async _createClearAuthorship(rev: any){
    83	    const atext = await this._pad.getInternalRevisionAText(rev);
    84	
    85	    // build clearAuthorship changeset
    86	    const builder = new Builder(atext.text.length);
    87	    builder.keepText(atext.text, [['author', '']], this._pad.pool);
    88	    const changeset = builder.toString();
    89	
    90	    return changeset;
    91	  }
    92	
    93	  async _createClearStartAtext(rev: any){
    94	    // get the atext of this revision
    95	    const atext = await this._pad.getInternalRevisionAText(rev);
    96	
    97	    // create the clearAuthorship changeset
    98	    const changeset = await this._createClearAuthorship(rev);
    99	
   100	    // apply the clearAuthorship changeset
   101	    const newAText = applyToAText(changeset, atext, this._pad.pool);
   102	
   103	    return newAText;
   104	  }
   105	  async _getChangesetsInBulk(startRev: any, count: any) {
   106	    // find out which revisions we need
   107	    const revisions = [];
   108	    for (let i = startRev; i < (startRev + count) && i <= this._pad.head; i++) {
   109	      revisions.push(i);
   110	    }
   111	
   112	    // get all needed revisions (in parallel)
   113	    const changesets:any[] = [];
   114	    const authors: any[] = [];
   115	    await Promise.all(revisions.map((rev) => this._pad.getRevision(rev).then((revision) => {
   116	      const arrayNum = rev - startRev;
   117	      changesets[arrayNum] = revision.changeset;
   118	      authors[arrayNum] = revision.meta.author;
   119	    })));
   120	
   121	    return {changesets, authors};
   122	  }
   123	  _addAuthors(authors: PadAuthor[]){
   124	      this.self = this;
   125	
   126	    // add to array if not in the array
   127	    authors.forEach((author) => {
   128	      if (this.self!._authors.indexOf(author) === -1) {
   129	        this.self!._authors.push(author);
   130	      }
   131	    });
   132	  }
   133	  async _createDiffAtext(){
   134	    const bulkSize = 100;
   135	
   136	    // get the cleaned startAText
   137	    let atext = await this._createClearStartAtext(this._fromRev);
   138	
   139	    let superChangeset = null;
   140	
   141	    for (let rev = this._fromRev + 1; rev <= this._toRev; rev += bulkSize) {
   142	      // get the bulk
   143	      const {changesets, authors} = await this._getChangesetsInBulk(rev, bulkSize);
   144	
   145	      const addedAuthors = [];
   146	
   147	      // run through all changesets
   148	      for (let i = 0; i < changesets.length && (rev + i) <= this._toRev; ++i) {
   149	        let changeset = changesets[i];
   150	
   151	        // skip clearAuthorship Changesets
   152	        if (this._isClearAuthorship(changeset)) {
   153	          continue;
   154	        }
   155	
   156	        changeset = this._extendChangesetWithAuthor(changeset, authors[i], this._pad.pool);
   157	
   158	        // add this author to the authorarray
   159	        addedAuthors.push(authors[i]);
   160	
   161	        // compose it with the superChangset
   162	        if (superChangeset == null) {
   163	          superChangeset = changeset;
   164	        } else {
   165	          superChangeset = compose(superChangeset, changeset, this._pad.pool);
   166	        }
   167	      }
   168	
   169	      // add the authors to the PadDiff authorArray
   170	      this._addAuthors(addedAuthors);
   171	    }
   172	
   173	    // if there are only clearAuthorship changesets, we don't get a superChangeset,
   174	    // so we can skip this step
   175	    if (superChangeset) {
   176	      const deletionChangeset = this._createDeletionChangeset(superChangeset, atext, this._pad.pool);
   177	
   178	      // apply the superChangeset, which includes all addings
   179	      atext = applyToAText(superChangeset, atext, this._pad.pool);
   180	
   181	      // apply the deletionChangeset, which adds a deletions
   182	      atext = applyToAText(deletionChangeset, atext, this._pad.pool);
   183	    }
   184	
   185	    return atext;
   186	  }
   187	  async getHtml(){
   188	    // cache the html
   189	    if (this._html != null) {
   190	      return this._html;
   191	    }
   192	
   193	    // get the diff atext
   194	    const atext = await this._createDiffAtext();
   195	
   196	    // get the authorColor table
   197	    const authorColors = await this._pad.getAllAuthorColors();
   198	
   199	    // convert the atext to html
   200	    this._html = await exportHtml.getHTMLFromAtext(this._pad, atext, authorColors);
   201	
   202	    return this._html;
   203	  }
   204	
   205	  async getAuthors() {
   206	    // check if html was already produced, if not produce it, this generates
   207	    // the author array at the same time
   208	    if (this._html == null) {
   209	      await this.getHtml();
   210	    }
   211	
   212	    return this.self!._authors;
   213	  }
   214	
   215	  _extendChangesetWithAuthor(changeset: any, author: any, apool: any){
   216	    // unpack
   217	    const unpacked = unpack(changeset);
   218	
   219	    const assem = new OpAssembler();
   220	
   221	    // create deleted attribs
   222	    const authorAttrib = apool.putAttrib(['author', author || '']);
   223	    const deletedAttrib = apool.putAttrib(['removed', true]);
   224	    const attribs = `*${numToString(authorAttrib)}*${numToString(deletedAttrib)}`;
   225	
   226	    for (const operator of deserializeOps(unpacked.ops)) {
   227	      if (operator.opcode === '-') {
   228	        // this is a delete operator, extend it with the author
   229	        operator.attribs = attribs;
   230	      } else if (operator.opcode === '=' && operator.attribs) {
   231	        // this is operator changes only attributes, let's mark which author did that
   232	        operator.attribs += `*${numToString(authorAttrib)}`;
   233	      }
   234	
   235	      // append the new operator to our assembler
   236	      assem.append(operator);
   237	    }
   238	
   239	    // return the modified changeset
   240	    return pack(unpacked.oldLen, unpacked.newLen, assem.toString(), unpacked.charBank);
   241	  }
   242	  _createDeletionChangeset(cs: any, startAText: any, apool: any){
   243	    const lines = splitTextLines(startAText.text);
   244	    const alines = splitAttributionLines(startAText.attribs, startAText.text);
   245	
   246	    // lines and alines are what the exports is meant to apply to.
   247	    // They may be arrays or objects with .get(i) and .length methods.
   248	    // They include final newlines on lines.
   249	
   250	    const linesGet = (idx: number) => {
   251	      // @ts-ignore
   252	      if (lines.get) {
   253	        // @ts-ignore
   254	        return lines.get(idx);
   255	      } else {
   256	        // @ts-ignore
   257	        return lines[idx];
   258	      }
   259	    };
   260	
   261	    const aLinesGet = (idx: number) => {
   262	      // @ts-ignore
   263	      if (alines.get) {
   264	        // @ts-ignore
   265	        return alines.get(idx);
   266	      } else {
   267	        return alines[idx];
   268	      }
   269	    };
   270	
   271	    let curLine = 0;
   272	    let curChar = 0;
   273	    let curLineOps: { next: () => any; } | null = null;
   274	    let curLineOpsNext: { done: any; value: any; } | null = null;
   275	    let curLineOpsLine: number;
   276	    let curLineNextOp = new Op('+');
   277	
   278	    const unpacked = unpack(cs);
   279	    const builder = new Builder(unpacked.newLen);
   280	
   281	    const consumeAttribRuns = (numChars: number, func: Function /* (len, attribs, endsLine)*/) => {
   282	      if (!curLineOps || curLineOpsLine !== curLine) {
   283	        curLineOps = deserializeOps(aLinesGet(curLine));
   284	        curLineOpsNext = curLineOps!.next();
   285	        curLineOpsLine = curLine;
   286	        let indexIntoLine = 0;
   287	        while (!curLineOpsNext!.done) {
   288	          curLineNextOp = curLineOpsNext!.value;
   289	          curLineOpsNext = curLineOps!.next();
   290	          if (indexIntoLine + curLineNextOp.chars >= curChar) {
   291	            curLineNextOp.chars -= (curChar - indexIntoLine);
   292	            break;
   293	          }
   294	          indexIntoLine += curLineNextOp.chars;
   295	        }
   296	      }
   297	
   298	      while (numChars > 0) {
   299	        if (!curLineNextOp.chars && curLineOpsNext!.done) {
   300	          curLine++;
   301	          curChar = 0;
   302	          curLineOpsLine = curLine;
   303	          curLineNextOp.chars = 0;
   304	          curLineOps = deserializeOps(aLinesGet(curLine));
   305	          curLineOpsNext = curLineOps!.next();
   306	        }
   307	
   308	        if (!curLineNextOp.chars) {
   309	          if (curLineOpsNext!.done) {
   310	            curLineNextOp = new Op();
   311	          } else {
   312	            curLineNextOp = curLineOpsNext!.value;
   313	            curLineOpsNext = curLineOps!.next();
   314	          }
   315	        }
   316	
   317	        const charsToUse = Math.min(numChars, curLineNextOp.chars);
   318	
   319	        func(charsToUse, curLineNextOp.attribs,
   320	            charsToUse === curLineNextOp.chars && curLineNextOp.lines > 0);
   321	        numChars -= charsToUse;
   322	        curLineNextOp.chars -= charsToUse;
   323	        curChar += charsToUse;
   324	      }
   325	
   326	      if (!curLineNextOp.chars && curLineOpsNext!.done) {
   327	        curLine++;
   328	        curChar = 0;
   329	      }
   330	    };
   331	
   332	    const skip = (N:number, L:number) => {
   333	      if (L) {
   334	        curLine += L;
   335	        curChar = 0;
   336	      } else if (curLineOps && curLineOpsLine === curLine) {
   337	        consumeAttribRuns(N, () => {});
   338	      } else {
   339	        curChar += N;
   340	      }
   341	    };
   342	
   343	    const nextText = (numChars: number) => {
   344	      let len = 0;
   345	      const assem = new StringAssembler();
   346	      const firstString = linesGet(curLine).substring(curChar);
   347	      len += firstString.length;
   348	      assem.append(firstString);
   349	
   350	      let lineNum = curLine + 1;
   351	
   352	      while (len < numChars) {
   353	        const nextString = linesGet(lineNum);
   354	        len += nextString.length;
   355	        assem.append(nextString);
   356	        lineNum++;
   357	      }
   358	
   359	      return assem.toString().substring(0, numChars);
   360	    };
   361	
   362	    const cachedStrFunc = (func:Function) => {
   363	      const cache:MapArrayType<any> = {};
   364	
   365	      return (s:string) => {
   366	        if (!cache[s]) {
   367	          cache[s] = func(s);
   368	        }
   369	        return cache[s];
   370	      };
   371	    };
   372	
   373	    for (const csOp of deserializeOps(unpacked.ops)) {
   374	      if (csOp.opcode === '=') {
   375	        const textBank = nextText(csOp.chars);
   376	
   377	        // decide if this equal operator is an attribution change or not.
   378	        // We can see this by checkinf if attribs is set.
   379	        // If the text this operator applies to is only a star,
   380	        // than this is a false positive and should be ignored
   381	        if (csOp.attribs && textBank !== '*') {
   382	          const attribs = AttributeMap.fromString(csOp.attribs, apool);
   383	          const undoBackToAttribs = cachedStrFunc((oldAttribsStr: string) => {
   384	            const oldAttribs = AttributeMap.fromString(oldAttribsStr, apool);
   385	            const backAttribs = new AttributeMap(apool)
   386	                .set('author', '')
   387	                .set('removed', 'true');
   388	            for (const [key, value] of attribs) {
   389	              const oldValue = oldAttribs.get(key);
   390	              if (oldValue !== value) backAttribs.set(key, oldValue);
   391	            }
   392	            // TODO: backAttribs does not restore removed attributes (it is missing attributes that
   393	            // are in oldAttribs but not in attribs). I don't know if that is intentional.
   394	            return backAttribs.toString();
   395	          });
   396	
   397	          let textLeftToProcess = textBank;
   398	
   399	          while (textLeftToProcess.length > 0) {
   400	            // process till the next line break or process only one line break
   401	            let lengthToProcess = textLeftToProcess.indexOf('\n');
   402	            let lineBreak = false;
   403	            switch (lengthToProcess) {
   404	              case -1:
   405	                lengthToProcess = textLeftToProcess.length;
   406	                break;
   407	              case 0:
   408	                lineBreak = true;
   409	                lengthToProcess = 1;
   410	                break;
   411	            }
   412	
   413	            // get the text we want to procceed in this step
   414	            const processText = textLeftToProcess.substr(0, lengthToProcess);
   415	
   416	            textLeftToProcess = textLeftToProcess.substr(lengthToProcess);
   417	
   418	            if (lineBreak) {
   419	              builder.keep(1, 1); // just skip linebreaks, don't do a insert + keep for a linebreak
   420	
   421	              // consume the attributes of this linebreak
   422	              consumeAttribRuns(1, () => {});
   423	            } else {
   424	              // add the old text via an insert, but add a deletion attribute +
   425	              // the author attribute of the author who deleted it
   426	              let textBankIndex = 0;
   427	              consumeAttribRuns(lengthToProcess, (len: number, attribs:string, endsLine: string) => {
   428	                // get the old attributes back
   429	                const oldAttribs = undoBackToAttribs(attribs);
   430	
   431	                builder.insert(processText.substr(textBankIndex, len), oldAttribs);
   432	                textBankIndex += len;
   433	              });
   434	
   435	              builder.keep(lengthToProcess, 0);
   436	            }
   437	          }
   438	        } else {
   439	          skip(csOp.chars, csOp.lines);
   440	          builder.keep(csOp.chars, csOp.lines);
   441	        }
   442	      } else if (csOp.opcode === '+') {
   443	        builder.keep(csOp.chars, csOp.lines);
   444	      } else if (csOp.opcode === '-') {
   445	        const textBank = nextText(csOp.chars);
   446	        let textBankIndex = 0;
   447	
   448	        consumeAttribRuns(csOp.chars, (len: number, attribs: string[], endsLine: string) => {
   449	          builder.insert(textBank.substr(textBankIndex, len), attribs + csOp.attribs);
   450	          textBankIndex += len;
   451	        });
   452	      }
   453	    }
   454	
   455	    return checkRep(builder.toString());
   456	  }
   457	
   458	}
   459	
   460	
   461	// this method is 80% like Changeset.inverse. I just changed so instead of reverting,
   462	// it adds deletions and attribute changes to the atext.
   463	// @ts-ignore
   464	PadDiff.prototype._createDeletionChangeset = function (cs, startAText, apool) {
   465	
   466	};
   467	
   468	// export the constructor
   469	module.exports = PadDiff;
   470

    19	/*
    20	 * This is the Changeset library copied from the old Etherpad with some modifications
    21	 * to use it in node.js. The original can be found at:
    22	 * https://github.com/ether/pad/blob/master/infrastructure/ace/www/easysync2.js

    25	import AttributeMap from './AttributeMap'
    26	import AttributePool from "./AttributePool";
    27	import {attribsFromString} from './attributes';
    28	import padutils from "./pad_utils";
    29	import Op, {OpCode} from './Op'
    30	import {numToString, parseNum} from './ChangesetUtils'
    31	import {StringAssembler} from "./StringAssembler";
    32	import {OpIter} from "./OpIter";
    33	import {Attribute} from "./types/Attribute";
    34	import {SmartOpAssembler} from "./SmartOpAssembler";
    35	import TextLinesMutator from "./TextLinesMutator";
    36	import {ChangeSet} from "./types/ChangeSet";
    37	import {AText} from "./types/AText";
    38	import {ChangeSetBuilder} from "./types/ChangeSetBuilder";

    40	import {StringIterator} from "./StringIterator";
    41	import {MergingOpAssembler} from "./MergingOpAssembler";
    42	
    43	/**
    44	 * A `[key, value]` pair of strings describing a text attribute.
    45	 *
    46	 * @typedef {[string, string]} Attribute
    47	 */
    48	
    49	/**
    50	 * A concatenated sequence of zero or more attribute identifiers, each one represented by an
    51	 * asterisk followed by a base-36 encoded attribute number.
    52	 *
    53	 * Examples: '', '*0', '*3*j*z*1q'
    54	 *
    55	 * @typedef {string} AttributeString
    56	 */
    57	
    58	/**
    59	 * This method is called whenever there is an error in the sync process.
    60	 *
    61	 * @param {string} msg - Just some message
    62	 */
    63	const error = (msg: string) => {
    64	  const e = new Error(msg);
    65	  // @ts-ignore
    66	  e.easysync = true;
    67	  throw e;
    68	};
    69	
    70	/**
    71	 * Assert that a condition is truthy. If the condition is falsy, the `error` function is called to
    72	 * throw an exception.
    73	 *
    74	 * @param {boolean} b - assertion condition
    75	 * @param {string} msg - error message to include in the exception
    76	 * @type {(b: boolean, msg: string) => asserts b}
    77	 */
    78	export const assert: (b: boolean, msg: string) => asserts b = (b: boolean, msg: string): asserts b => {
    79	  if (!b) error(`Failed assertion: ${msg}`);
    80	};
    81	
    82	
    83	/**
    84	 * Describes changes to apply to a document. Does not include the attribute pool or the original
    85	 * document.
    86	 *
    87	 * @typedef {object} Changeset
    88	 * @property {number} oldLen - The length of the base document.
    89	 * @property {number} newLen - The length of the document after applying the changeset.
    90	 * @property {string} ops - Serialized sequence of operations. Use `deserializeOps` to parse this
    91	 *     string.
    92	 * @property {string} charBank - Characters inserted by insert operations.
    93	 */
    94	
    95	/**
    96	 * Returns the required length of the text before changeset can be applied.
    97	 *
    98	 * @param {string} cs - String representation of the Changeset
    99	 * @returns {number} oldLen property
   100	 */
   101	export const oldLen = (cs: string) => unpack(cs).oldLen
   102	
   103	/**
   104	 * Returns the length of the text after changeset is applied.
   105	 *
   106	 * @param {string} cs - String representation of the Changeset
   107	 * @returns {number} newLen property
   108	 */
   109	export const newLen = (cs: string) => unpack(cs).newLen
   110	
   111	/**
   112	 * Parses a string of serialized changeset operations.
   113	 *
   114	 * @param {string} ops - Serialized changeset operations.
   115	 * @yields {Op}
   116	 * @returns {Generator<Op>}
   117	 */
   118	export const deserializeOps = function* (ops: string) {
   119	  // TODO: Migrate to String.prototype.matchAll() once there is enough browser support.
   120	  const regex = /((?:\*[0-9a-z]+)*)(?:\|([0-9a-z]+))?([-+=])([0-9a-z]+)|(.)/g;
   121	  let match;
   122	  while ((match = regex.exec(ops)) != null) {
   123	    if (match[5] === '$') return; // Start of the insert operation character bank.
   124	    if (match[5] != null) error(`invalid operation: ${ops.slice(regex.lastIndex - 1)}`);
   125	    const opMatch = match[3] as   ""|"=" | "+" | "-" | undefined
   126	    const op = new Op(opMatch);
   127	    op.lines = parseNum(match[2] || '0');
   128	    op.chars = parseNum(match[4]);
   129	    op.attribs = match[1];
   130	    yield op;
   131	  }
   132	};
   133	
   134	
   135	
   136	/**
   137	 * Creates an iterator which decodes string changeset operations.
   138	 *
   139	 * @deprecated Use `deserializeOps` instead.
   140	 * @param {string} opsStr - String encoding of the change operations to perform.
   141	 * @returns {OpIter} Operator iterator object.
   142	 */
   143	export const opIterator = (opsStr: string) => {
   144	  padutils.warnDeprecated(
   145	    'Changeset.opIterator() is deprecated; use Changeset.deserializeOps() instead');
   146	  return new OpIter(opsStr);
   147	};
   148	
   149	/**
   150	 * Cleans an Op object.
   151	 *
   152	 * @param {Op} op - object to clear
   153	 */
   154	export const clearOp = (op: Op) => {
   155	  op.opcode = '';
   156	  op.chars = 0;
   157	  op.lines = 0;
   158	  op.attribs = '';
   159	};
   160	
   161	/**
   162	 * Creates a new Op object
   163	 *
   164	 * @deprecated Use the `Op` class instead.
   165	 * @param {('+'|'-'|'='|'')} [optOpcode=''] - The operation's operator.
   166	 * @returns {Op}
   167	 */
   168	export const newOp = (optOpcode:'+'|'-'|'='|'' ): Op => {
   169	  padutils.warnDeprecated('Changeset.newOp() is deprecated; use the Changeset.Op class instead');
   170	  return new Op(optOpcode);
   171	};
   172	
   173	/**
   174	 * Copies op1 to op2
   175	 *
   176	 * @param {Op} op1 - src Op
   177	 * @param {Op} [op2] - dest Op. If not given, a new Op is used.
   178	 * @returns {Op} `op2`
   179	 */
   180	export const copyOp = (op1: Op, op2: Op = new Op()): Op => Object.assign(op2, op1);
   181	
   182	/**
   183	 * Serializes a sequence of Ops.
   184	 *
   185	 * @typedef {object} OpAssembler
   186	 * @property {Function} append -
   187	 * @property {Function} clear -
   188	 * @property {Function} toString -
   189	 */
   190	
   191	/**
   192	 * Efficiently merges consecutive operations that are mergeable, ignores no-ops, and drops final
   193	 * pure "keeps". It does not re-order operations.
   194	 *
   195	 * @typedef {object} MergingOpAssembler
   196	 * @property {Function} append -
   197	 * @property {Function} clear -
   198	 * @property {Function} endDocument -
   199	 * @property {Function} toString -
   200	 */
   201	
   202	/**
   203	 * Generates operations from the given text and attributes.
   204	 *
   205	 * @param {('-'|'+'|'=')} opcode - The operator to use.
   206	 * @param {string} text - The text to remove/add/keep.
   207	 * @param {(Iterable<Attribute>|AttributeString)} [attribs] - The attributes to insert into the pool
   208	 *     (if necessary) and encode. If an attribute string, no checking is performed to ensure that
   209	 *     the attributes exist in the pool, are in the canonical order, and contain no duplicate keys.
   210	 *     If this is an iterable of attributes, `pool` must be non-null.
   211	 * @param {?AttributePool.ts} pool - Attribute pool. Required if `attribs` is an iterable of
   212	 *     attributes, ignored if `attribs` is an attribute string.
   213	 * @yields {Op} One or two ops (depending on the presense of newlines) that cover the given text.
   214	 * @returns {Generator<Op>}
   215	 */
   216	export const opsFromText = function* (opcode: "" | "=" | "+" | "-" | undefined, text: string, attribs: string|Attribute[] = '', pool: AttributePool|null = null) {
   217	  const op = new Op(opcode);
   218	  op.attribs = typeof attribs === 'string'
   219	    ? attribs : new AttributeMap(pool).update(attribs || [], opcode === '+').toString();
   220	  const lastNewlinePos = text.lastIndexOf('\n');
   221	  if (lastNewlinePos < 0) {
   222	    op.chars = text.length;
   223	    op.lines = 0;
   224	    yield op;
   225	  } else {
   226	    op.chars = lastNewlinePos + 1;
   227	    op.lines = text.match(/\n/g)!.length;
   228	    yield op;
   229	    const op2 = copyOp(op);
   230	    op2.chars = text.length - (lastNewlinePos + 1);
   231	    op2.lines = 0;
   232	    yield op2;
   233	  }
   234	};
   235	
   236	
   237	
   238	/**
   239	 * Used to check if a Changeset is valid. This function does not check things that require access to
   240	 * the attribute pool (e.g., attribute order) or original text (e.g., newline positions).
   241	 *
   242	 * @param {string} cs - Changeset to check
   243	 * @returns {string} the checked Changeset
   244	 */
   245	export const checkRep = (cs: string) => {
   246	  const unpacked = unpack(cs);
   247	  const oldLen = unpacked.oldLen;
   248	  const newLen = unpacked.newLen;
   249	  const ops = unpacked.ops;
   250	  let charBank = unpacked.charBank;
   251	
   252	  const assem = new SmartOpAssembler();
   253	  let oldPos = 0;
   254	  let calcNewLen = 0;
   255	  for (const o of deserializeOps(ops)) {
   256	    switch (o.opcode) {
   257	      case '=':
   258	        oldPos += o.chars;
   259	        calcNewLen += o.chars;
   260	        break;
   261	      case '-':
   262	        oldPos += o.chars;
   263	        assert(oldPos <= oldLen, `${oldPos} > ${oldLen} in ${cs}`);
   264	        break;
   265	      case '+':
   266	      {
   267	        assert(charBank.length >= o.chars, 'Invalid changeset: not enough chars in charBank');
   268	        const chars = charBank.slice(0, o.chars);
   269	        const nlines = (chars.match(/\n/g) || []).length;
   270	        assert(nlines === o.lines,
   271	          'Invalid changeset: number of newlines in insert op does not match the charBank');
   272	        assert(o.lines === 0 || chars.endsWith('\n'),
   273	          'Invalid changeset: multiline insert op does not end with a newline');
   274	        charBank = charBank.slice(o.chars);
   275	        calcNewLen += o.chars;
   276	        assert(calcNewLen <= newLen, `${calcNewLen} > ${newLen} in ${cs}`);
   277	        break;
   278	      }
   279	      default:
   280	        assert(false, `Invalid changeset: Unknown opcode: ${JSON.stringify(o.opcode)}`);
   281	    }
   282	    assem.append(o);
   283	  }
   284	  calcNewLen += oldLen - oldPos;
   285	  assert(calcNewLen === newLen, 'Invalid changeset: claimed length does not match actual length');
   286	  assert(charBank === '', 'Invalid changeset: excess characters in the charBank');
   287	  assem.endDocument();
   288	  const normalized = pack(oldLen, calcNewLen, assem.toString(), unpacked.charBank);
   289	  assert(normalized === cs, 'Invalid changeset: not in canonical form');
   290	  return cs;
   291	};
   292	
   293	/**
   294	 * A custom made StringBuffer
   295	 *
   296	 * @typedef {object} StringAssembler
   297	 * @property {Function} append -
   298	 * @property {Function} toString -
   299	 */
   300	
   301	/**
   302	 * @typedef {object} StringArrayLike
   303	 * @property {(i: number) => string} get - Returns the line at index `i`.
   304	 * @property {(number|(() => number))} length - The number of lines, or a method that returns the
   305	 *     number of lines.
   306	 * @property {(((start?: number, end?: number) => string[])|undefined)} slice - Like
   307	 *     `Array.prototype.slice()`. Optional if the return value of the `removeLines` method is not
   308	 *     needed.
   309	 * @property {(i: number, d?: number, ...l: string[]) => any} splice - Like
   310	 *     `Array.prototype.splice()`.
   311	 */
   312	
   313	
   314	/**
   315	 * Apply operations to other operations.
   316	 *
   317	 * @param {string} in1 - first Op string
   318	 * @param {string} in2 - second Op string
   319	 * @param {Function} func - Callback that applies an operation to another operation. Will be called
   320	 *     multiple times depending on the number of operations in `in1` and `in2`. `func` has signature
   321	 *     `opOut = f(op1, op2)`:
   322	 *       - `op1` is the current operation from `in1`. `func` is expected to mutate `op1` to
   323	 *         partially or fully consume it, and MUST set `op1.opcode` to the empty string once `op1`
   324	 *         is fully consumed. If `op1` is not fully consumed, `func` will be called again with the
   325	 *         same `op1` value. If `op1` is fully consumed, the next call to `func` will be given the
   326	 *         next operation from `in1`. If there are no more operations in `in1`, `op1.opcode` will be
   327	 *         the empty string.
   328	 *       - `op2` is the current operation from `in2`, to apply to `op1`. Has the same consumption
   329	 *         and advancement semantics as `op1`.
   330	 *       - `opOut` is the result of applying `op2` (before consumption) to `op1` (before
   331	 *         consumption). If there is no result (perhaps `op1` and `op2` canceled each other out),
   332	 *         either `opOut` must be nullish or `opOut.opcode` must be the empty string.
   333	 * @returns {string} the integrated changeset
   334	 */
   335	const applyZip = (in1: string, in2: string, func: Function): string => {
   336	  const ops1 = deserializeOps(in1);
   337	  const ops2 = deserializeOps(in2);
   338	  let next1 = ops1.next();
   339	  let next2 = ops2.next();
   340	  const assem = new SmartOpAssembler();
   341	  while (!next1.done || !next2.done) {
   342	    if (!next1.done && !next1.value.opcode) next1 = ops1.next();
   343	    if (!next2.done && !next2.value.opcode) next2 = ops2.next();
   344	    if (next1.value == null) next1.value = new Op();
   345	    if (next2.value == null) next2.value = new Op();
   346	    if (!next1.value.opcode && !next2.value.opcode) break;
   347	    const opOut = func(next1.value, next2.value);
   348	    if (opOut && opOut.opcode) assem.append(opOut);
   349	  }
   350	  assem.endDocument();
   351	  return assem.toString();
   352	};
   353	
   354	/**
   355	 * Parses an encoded changeset.
   356	 *
   357	 * @param {string} cs - The encoded changeset.
   358	 * @returns {Changeset}
   359	 */
   360	export const unpack = (cs: string): ChangeSet => {
   361	  const headerRegex = /Z:([0-9a-z]+)([><])([0-9a-z]+)|/;
   362	  const headerMatch = headerRegex.exec(cs);
   363	  if ((!headerMatch) || (!headerMatch[0])) error(`Not a changeset: ${cs}`);
   364	  const oldLen = parseNum(headerMatch![1]);
   365	  const changeSign = (headerMatch![2] === '>') ? 1 : -1;
   366	  const changeMag = parseNum(headerMatch![3]);
   367	  const newLen = oldLen + changeSign * changeMag;
   368	  const opsStart = headerMatch![0].length;
   369	  let opsEnd = cs.indexOf('$');
   370	  if (opsEnd < 0) opsEnd = cs.length;
   371	  return {
   372	    oldLen,
   373	    newLen,
   374	    ops: cs.substring(opsStart, opsEnd),
   375	    charBank: cs.substring(opsEnd + 1),
   376	  };
   377	};
   378	
   379	/**
   380	 * Creates an encoded changeset.
   381	 *
   382	 * @param {number} oldLen - The length of the document before applying the changeset.
   383	 * @param {number} newLen - The length of the document after applying the changeset.
   384	 * @param {string} opsStr - Encoded operations to apply to the document.
   385	 * @param {string} bank - Characters for insert operations.
   386	 * @returns {string} The encoded changeset.
   387	 */
   388	export const pack = (oldLen: number, newLen: number, opsStr: string, bank: string): string => {
   389	  const lenDiff = newLen - oldLen;
   390	  const lenDiffStr = (lenDiff >= 0 ? `>${numToString(lenDiff)}`
   391	    : `<${numToString(-lenDiff)}`);
   392	  const a = [];
   393	  a.push('Z:', numToString(oldLen), lenDiffStr, opsStr, '$', bank);
   394	  return a.join('');
   395	};
   396	
   397	/**
   398	 * Applies a Changeset to a string.
   399	 *
   400	 * @param {string} cs - String encoded Changeset
   401	 * @param {string} str - String to which a Changeset should be applied
   402	 * @returns {string}
   403	 */
   404	export const applyToText = (cs: string, str: string): string => {
   405	  const unpacked = unpack(cs);
   406	  assert(str.length === unpacked.oldLen, `mismatched apply: ${str.length} / ${unpacked.oldLen}`);
   407	  const bankIter = new StringIterator(unpacked.charBank);
   408	  const strIter = new StringIterator(str);
   409	  const assem = new StringAssembler();
   410	  for (const op of deserializeOps(unpacked.ops)) {
   411	    switch (op.opcode) {
   412	      case '+':
   413	        // op is + and op.lines 0: no newlines must be in op.chars
   414	        // op is + and op.lines >0: op.chars must include op.lines newlines
   415	        if (op.lines !== bankIter.peek(op.chars).split('\n').length - 1) {
   416	          throw new Error(`newline count is wrong in op +; cs:${cs} and text:${str}`);
   417	        }
   418	        assem.append(bankIter.take(op.chars));
   419	        break;
   420	      case '-':
   421	        // op is - and op.lines 0: no newlines must be in the deleted string
   422	        // op is - and op.lines >0: op.lines newlines must be in the deleted string
   423	        if (op.lines !== strIter.peek(op.chars).split('\n').length - 1) {
   424	          throw new Error(`newline count is wrong in op -; cs:${cs} and text:${str}`);
   425	        }
   426	        strIter.skip(op.chars);
   427	        break;
   428	      case '=':
   429	        // op is = and op.lines 0: no newlines must be in the copied string
   430	        // op is = and op.lines >0: op.lines newlines must be in the copied string
   431	        if (op.lines !== strIter.peek(op.chars).split('\n').length - 1) {
   432	          throw new Error(`newline count is wrong in op =; cs:${cs} and text:${str}`);
   433	        }
   434	        assem.append(strIter.take(op.chars));
   435	        break;
   436	    }
   437	  }
   438	  assem.append(strIter.take(strIter.remaining()));
   439	  return <response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

   101	            "bold,true":3
   102	        },
   103	        "nextNum":4
   104	    }

   109	exports.getAttributePool = async (padID: string) => {
   110	  const pad = await getPadSafe(padID, true);
   111	  return {pool: pad.pool};
   112	};
   113	
   114	/**
   115	getRevisionChangeset (padID, [rev])
   116	
   117	get the changeset at a given revision, or last revision if 'rev' is not defined.
   118	
   119	Example returns:
   120	{
   121	    "code" : 0,
   122	    "message" : "ok",
   123	    "data" : "Z:1>6b|5+6b$Welcome to Etherpad!\n\nThis pad text is synchronized as you type, so that everyone viewing this page sees the same text. This allows you to collaborate seamlessly on documents!\n\nGet involved with Etherpad at http://etherpad.org\n"
   124	}
   125	
   126	*/
   127	exports.getRevisionChangeset = async (padID: string, rev: string) => {
   128	  // try to parse the revision number
   129	  if (rev !== undefined) {
   130	    rev = checkValidRev(rev);
   131	  }
   132	
   133	  // get the pad
   134	  const pad = await getPadSafe(padID, true);
   135	  const head = pad.getHeadRevisionNumber();
   136	
   137	  // the client asked for a special revision
   138	  if (rev !== undefined) {
   139	    // check if this is a valid revision
   140	    if (rev > head) {
   141	      throw new CustomError('rev is higher than the head revision of the pad', 'apierror');
   142	    }
   143	
   144	    // get the changeset for this revision
   145	    return await pad.getRevisionChangeset(rev);
   146	  }
   147	
   148	  // the client wants the latest changeset, lets return it to him
   149	  return await pad.getRevisionChangeset(head);
   150	};
   151	
   152	/**
   153	getText(padID, [rev]) returns the text of a pad
   154	
   155	Example returns:
   156	
   157	{code: 0, message:"ok", data: {text:"Welcome Text"}}
   158	{code: 1, message:"padID does not exist", data: null}
   159	*/
   160	exports.getText = async (padID: string, rev: string) => {
   161	  // try to parse the revision number
   162	  if (rev !== undefined) {
   163	    rev = checkValidRev(rev);
   164	  }
   165	
   166	  // get the pad
   167	  const pad = await getPadSafe(padID, true);
   168	  const head = pad.getHeadRevisionNumber();
   169	
   170	  // the client asked for a special revision
   171	  if (rev !== undefined) {
   172	    // check if this is a valid revision
   173	    if (rev > head) {
   174	      throw new CustomError('rev is higher than the head revision of the pad', 'apierror');
   175	    }
   176	
   177	    // get the text of this revision
   178	    // getInternalRevisionAText() returns an atext object, but we only want the .text inside it.
   179	    // Details at https://github.com/ether/etherpad-lite/issues/5073
   180	    const {text} = await pad.getInternalRevisionAText(rev);
   181	    return {text};
   182	  }
   183	
   184	  // the client wants the latest text, lets return it to him
   185	  const text = exportTxt.getTXTFromAtext(pad, pad.atext);
   186	  return {text};
   187	};
   188	
   189	/**
   190	setText(padID, text, [authorId]) sets the text of a pad
   191	
   192	Example returns:
   193	
   194	{code: 0, message:"ok", data: null}
   195	{code: 1, message:"padID does not exist", data: null}
   196	{code: 1, message:"text too long", data: null}

   198	/**
   199	 *
   200	 * @param {String} padID the id of the pad
   201	 * @param {String} text the text of the pad
   202	 * @param {String} authorId the id of the author, defaulting to empty string
   203	 * @returns {Promise<void>}
   204	 */
   205	exports.setText = async (padID: string, text?: string, authorId: string = ''): Promise<void> => {
   206	  // text is required
   207	  if (typeof text !== 'string') {
   208	    throw new CustomError('text is not a string', 'apierror');
   209	  }
   210	
   211	  // get the pad
   212	  const pad = await getPadSafe(padID, true);
   213	
   214	  await pad.setText(text, authorId);
   215	  await padMessageHandler.updatePadClients(pad);
   216	};
   217	
   218	/**
   219	appendText(padID, text, [authorId]) appends text to a pad
   220	
   221	Example returns:
   222	
   223	{code: 0, message:"ok", data: null}
   224	{code: 1, message:"padID does not exist", data: null}
   225	{code: 1, message:"text too long", data: null}
   226	 @param {String} padID the id of the pad
   227	 @param {String} text the text of the pad
   228	 @param {String} authorId the id of the author, defaulting to empty string
   229	 */
   230	exports.appendText = async (padID:string, text?: string, authorId:string = '') => {
   231	  // text is required
   232	  if (typeof text !== 'string') {
   233	    throw new CustomError('text is not a string', 'apierror');
   234	  }
   235	
   236	  const pad = await getPadSafe(padID, true);
   237	  await pad.appendText(text, authorId);
   238	  await padMessageHandler.updatePadClients(pad);
   239	};
   240	
   241	/**
   242	getHTML(padID, [rev]) returns the html of a pad
   243	
   244	Example returns:
   245	
   246	{code: 0, message:"ok", data: {text:"Welcome <strong>Text</strong>"}}
   247	{code: 1, message:"padID does not exist", data: null}
   248	 @param {String} padID the id of the pad
   249	 @param {String} rev the revision number, defaulting to the latest revision
   250	 @return {Promise<{html: string}>} the html of the pad
   251	*/
   252	exports.getHTML = async (padID: string, rev: string): Promise<{ html: string; }> => {
   253	  if (rev !== undefined) {
   254	    rev = checkValidRev(rev);
   255	  }
   256	
   257	  const pad = await getPadSafe(padID, true);
   258	
   259	  // the client asked for a special revision
   260	  if (rev !== undefined) {
   261	    // check if this is a valid revision
   262	    const head = pad.getHeadRevisionNumber();
   263	    if (rev > head) {
   264	      throw new CustomError('rev is higher than the head revision of the pad', 'apierror');
   265	    }
   266	  }
   267	
   268	  // get the html of this revision
   269	  let html = await exportHtml.getPadHTML(pad, rev);
   270	
   271	  // wrap the HTML
   272	  html = `<!DOCTYPE HTML><html><body>${html}</body></html>`;
   273	  return {html};
   274	};
   275	
   276	/**
   277	setHTML(padID, html, [authorId]) sets the text of a pad based on HTML
   278	
   279	Example returns:
   280	
   281	{code: 0, message:"ok", data: null}
   282	{code: 1, message:"padID does not exist", data: null}
   283	
   284	 @param {String} padID the id of the pad
   285	 @param {String} html the html of the pad
   286	 @param {String} authorId the id of the author, defaulting to empty string
   287	*/
   288	exports.setHTML = async (padID: string, html:string|object, authorId = '') => {
   289	  // html string is required
   290	  if (typeof html !== 'string') {
   291	    throw new CustomError('html is not a string', 'apierror');
   292	  }
   293	
   294	  // get the pad
   295	  const pad = await getPadSafe(padID, true);
   296	
   297	  // add a new changeset with the new html to the pad
   298	  try {
   299	    await importHtml.setPadHTML(pad, cleanText(html), authorId);
   300	  } catch (e) {
   301	    throw new CustomError('HTML is malformed', 'apierror');
   302	  }
   303	
   304	  // update the clients on the pad
   305	  padMessageHandler.updatePadClients(pad);
   306	};
   307	
   308	/* ****************
   309	 * CHAT FUNCTIONS *
   310	 **************** */
   311	
   312	/**
   313	getChatHistory(padId, start, end), returns a part of or the whole chat-history of this pad
   314	
   315	Example returns:
   316	
   317	{"code":0,"message":"ok","data":{"messages":[
   318	  {"text":"foo","authorID":"a.foo","time":1359199533759,"userName":"test"},
   319	  {"text":"bar","authorID":"a.foo","time":1359199534622,"userName":"test"}
   320	]}}
   321	
   322	{code: 1, message:"start is higher or equal to the current chatHead", data: null}
   323	
   324	{code: 1, message:"padID does not exist", data: null}
   325	 @param {String} padID the id of the pad
   326	 @param {Number} start the start point of the chat-history
   327	 @param {Number} end the end point of the chat-history
   328	*/
   329	exports.getChatHistory = async (padID: string, start:number, end:number) => {
   330	  if (start && end) {
   331	    if (start < 0) {
   332	      throw new CustomError('start is below zero', 'apierror');
   333	    }
   334	    if (end < 0) {
   335	      throw new CustomError('end is below zero', 'apierror');
   336	    }
   337	    if (start > end) {
   338	      throw new CustomError('start is higher than end', 'apierror');
   339	    }
   340	  }
   341	
   342	  // get the pad
   343	  const pad = await getPadSafe(padID, true);
   344	
   345	  const chatHead = pad.chatHead;
   346	
   347	  // fall back to getting the whole chat-history if a parameter is missing
   348	  if (!start || !end) {
   349	    start = 0;
   350	    end = pad.chatHead;
   351	  }
   352	
   353	  if (start > chatHead) {
   354	    throw new CustomError('start is higher than the current chatHead', 'apierror');
   355	  }
   356	  if (end > chatHead) {
   357	    throw new CustomError('end is higher than the current chatHead', 'apierror');
   358	  }
   359	
   360	  // the whole message-log and return it to the client
   361	  const messages = await pad.getChatMessages(start, end);
   362	
   363	  return {messages};
   364	};
   365	
   366	/**
   367	appendChatMessage(padID, text, authorID, time), creates a chat message for the pad id,
   368	time is a timestamp
   369	
   370	Example returns:
   371	
   372	{code: 0, message:"ok", data: null}
   373	{code: 1, message:"padID does not exist", data: null}
   374	 @param {String} padID the id of the pad
   375	 @param {String} text the text of the chat-message
   376	 @param {String} authorID the id of the author
   377	 @param {Number} time the timestamp of the chat-message
   378	*/
   379	exports.appendChatMessage = async (padID: string, text: string|object, authorID: string, time: number) => {
   380	  // text is required
   381	  if (typeof text !== 'string') {
   382	    throw new CustomError('text is not a string', 'apierror');
   383	  }
   384	
   385	  // if time is not an integer value set time to current timestamp
   386	  if (time === undefined || !isInt(time)) {
   387	    time = Date.now();
   388	  }
   389	
   390	  // @TODO - missing getPadSafe() call ?
   391	
   392	  // save chat message to database and send message to all connected clients
   393	  await padMessageHandler.sendChatMessageToPadClients(new ChatMessage(text, authorID, time), padID);
   394	};
   395

   453	     @param {Number} rev the revision number, defaulting to the latest revision
   454	*/
   455	exports.saveRevision = async (padID: string, rev: number) => {
   456	  // check if rev is a number
   457	  if (rev !== undefined) {
   458	    rev = checkValidRev(rev);
   459	  }
   460	
   461	  // get the pad
   462	  const pad = await getPadSafe(padID, true);
   463	  const head = pad.getHeadRevisionNumber();
   464	
   465	  // the client asked for a special revision
   466	  if (rev !== undefined) {
   467	    if (rev > head) {
   468	      throw new CustomError('rev is higher than the head revision of the pad', 'apierror');
   469	    }
   470	  } else {
   471	    rev = pad.getHeadRevisionNumber();
   472	  }
   473	
   474	  const author = await authorManager.createAuthor('API');
   475	  await pad.addSavedRevision(rev, author.authorID, 'Saved through API call');
   476	};

   495	/**
   496	createPad(padName, [text], [authorId]) creates a new pad in this group
   497	
   498	Example returns:
   499	
   500	{code: 0, message:"ok", data: null}
   501	{code: 1, message:"pad does already exist", data: null}
   502	 @param {String} padID the name of the new pad
   503	    @param {String} text the initial text of the pad
   504	     @param {String} authorId the id of the author, defaulting to empty string
   505	*/
   506	exports.createPad = async (padID: string, text: string, authorId = '') => {
   507	  if (padID) {
   508	    // ensure there is no $ in the padID
   509	    if (padID.indexOf('$') !== -1) {
   510	      throw new CustomError("createPad can't create group pads", 'apierror');
   511	    }
   512	
   513	    // check for url special characters
   514	    if (padID.match(/(\/|\?|&|#)/)) {
   515	      throw new CustomError('malformed padID: Remove special characters', 'apierror');
   516	    }
   517	  }
   518	
   519	  // create pad
   520	  await getPadSafe(padID, false, text, authorId);
   521	};

Now let me look at PadDiff._createDiffAtext more carefully and the _getChangesetsInBulk method. The issue is with `startRev` and `endRev` being the same. Let's trace through the logic:

1. `_createDiffAtext` gets `_createClearStartAtext(this._fromRev)` 
2. Then loops from `this._fromRev + 1` to `this._toRev`
3. If `_fromRev === _toRev`, the loop doesn't execute
4. `superChangeset` stays null
5. The function returns `atext` (the clear start atext)

But wait - the error trace says "Not a changeset: undefined". Let's check what happens in `_createClearStartAtext`:

1. `getInternalRevisionAText(rev)` - gets the atext at a revision
2. `_createClearAuthorship(rev)` - builds a clearAuthorship changeset
3. `applyToAText(changeset, atext, pool)` - applies it

   208	  async getInternalRevisionAText(targetRev: number) {
   209	    const keyRev = this.getKeyRevisionNumber(targetRev);
   210	    const headRev = this.getHeadRevisionNumber();
   211	    if (targetRev > headRev) targetRev = headRev;
   212	    const [keyAText, changesets] = await Promise.all([
   213	      this._getKeyRevisionAText(keyRev),
   214	      Promise.all(
   215	          Stream.range(keyRev + 1, targetRev + 1).map(this.getRevisionChangeset.bind(this))),
   216	    ]);
   217	    const apool = this.apool();
   218	    let atext = keyAText;
   219	    for (const cs of changesets) atext = applyToAText(cs, atext, apool);

   241	  getValidRevisionRange(startRev: any, endRev:any) {
   242	    startRev = parseInt(startRev, 10);
   243	    const head = this.getHeadRevisionNumber();
   244	    endRev = endRev ? parseInt(endRev, 10) : head;
   245	
   246	    if (isNaN(startRev) || startRev < 0 || startRev > head) {
   247	      startRev = null;
   248	    }
   249	
   250	    if (isNaN(endRev) || endRev < startRev) {
   251	      endRev = null;
   252	    } else if (endRev > head) {
   253	      endRev = head;
   254	    }
   255	
   256	    if (startRev != null && endRev != null) {
   257	      return {startRev, endRev};
   258	    }
   259	    return null;
   260	  }
   261	
   262	  getKeyRevisionNumber(revNum: number) {
   263	    return Math.floor(revNum / 100) * 100;
   264	  }

Now I see! `getRevision` returns the whole revision object, but `getRevisionChangeset` returns just the changeset string. Let me check how `_getChangesetsInBulk` uses `getRevision`:

Looking at padDiff.ts lines 105-121:
```javascript
async _getChangesetsInBulk(startRev: any, count: any) {
    const revisions = [];
    for (let i = startRev; i < (startRev + count) && i <= this._pad.head; i++) {
      revisions.push(i);
    }

    const changesets:any[] = [];
    const authors: any[] = [];
    await Promise.all(revisions.map((rev) => this._pad.getRevision(rev).then((revision) => {
      const arrayNum = rev - startRev;
      changesets[arrayNum] = revision.changeset;
      authors[arrayNum] = revision.meta.author;
    })));

    return {changesets, authors};
  }
```

So `getRevision` returns a revision object with `changeset` and `meta.author`. This should work...

But wait, let me look at the error trace again:

OK so `applyToAText` calls `applyToText(cs, atext.text)` and `applyToText` calls `unpack(cs)`. The error "Not a changeset: undefined" means `cs` is `undefined`.

So the question is: where is `undefined` being passed as a changeset?

Let me look at `_createClearAuthorship`:

```javascript
async _createClearAuthorship(rev: any){
    const atext = await this._pad.getInternalRevisionAText(rev);

    // build clearAuthorship changeset
    const builder = new Builder(atext.text.length);
    builder.keepText(atext.text, [['author', '']], this._pad.pool);
    const changeset = builder.toString();

    return changeset;
  }
```

This should return a valid changeset. But wait, `rev` could be `undefined`? No, `_fromRev` is set from `getValidRevisionRange`.

Let me look more carefully at `getValidRevisionRange`:

```javascript
getValidRevisionRange(startRev: any, endRev:any) {
    startRev = parseInt(startRev, 10);
    const head = this.getHeadRevisionNumber();
    endRev = endRev ? parseInt(endRev, 10) : head;

    if (isNaN(startRev) || startRev < 0 || startRev > head) {
      startRev = null;
    }

    if (isNaN(endRev) || endRev < startRev) {
      endRev = null;
    } else if (endRev > head) {
      endRev = head;
    }

    if (startRev != null && endRev != null) {
      return {startRev, endRev};
    }
    return null;
  }
```

Notice: `endRev = endRev ? parseInt(endRev, 10) : head;` - if `endRev` is 0, this evaluates to `head`! Because `0` is falsy in JavaScript.

But also, `startRev` could be 0. Let's check what happens for `startRev=1`. For `startRev=1`, `endRev=1`:

- `startRev = parseInt("1", 10)` = 1
- `endRev = 1 ? parseInt(1, 10) : head` = 1 (since 1 is truthy)

Wait, but the PR description says it fails even when startRev and endRev are the same. Let me look at the error more carefully.


'use strict';

import {PadType} from "../../../../node/types/PadType";

const assert = require('assert').strict;
const authorManager = require('../../../../node/db/AuthorManager');
const common = require('../../common');
const padManager = require('../../../../node/db/PadManager');

describe(__filename, function () {
  let agent:any;
  let authorId: string;
  let padId: string;
  let pad: PadType;

  const restoreRevision = async (v:string, padId: string, rev: number, authorId:string|null = null) => {
    // @ts-ignore
    const p = new URLSearchParams(Object.entries({
      padID: padId,
      rev,
      ...(authorId == null ? {} : {authorId}),
    }));
    const res = await agent.get(`/api/${v}/restoreRevision?${p}`)
        .set("Authorization", (await common.generateJWTToken()))
        .expect(200)
        .expect('Content-Type', /json/);
    assert.equal(res.body.code, 0);
  };

  before(async function () {
    agent = await common.init();
    authorId = await authorManager.getAuthor4Token('test-restoreRevision');
    assert(authorId);
  });

  beforeEach(async function () {
    padId = common.randomString();
    if (await padManager.doesPadExist(padId)) await padManager.removePad(padId);
    pad = await padManager.getPad(padId);
    await pad.appendText('\nfoo');
    await pad.appendText('\nbar');
    assert.equal(pad.head, 2);
  });

  afterEach(async function () {
    if (await padManager.doesPadExist(padId)) await padManager.removePad(padId);
  });

  describe('v1.2.11', function () {
    // TODO: Enable once the end-of-pad newline bugs are fixed. See:
    // https://github.com/ether/etherpad-lite/pull/5253
    xit('content matches', async function () {
      const oldHead = pad.head;
      const wantAText = await pad.getInternalRevisionAText(pad.head - 1);
      assert(wantAText.text.endsWith('\nfoo\n'));
      await restoreRevision('1.2.11', padId, pad.head - 1);
      assert.equal(pad.head, oldHead + 1);
      assert.deepEqual(await pad.getInternalRevisionAText(pad.head), wantAText);
    });

    it('authorId ignored', async function () {
      const oldHead = pad.head;
      await restoreRevision('1.2.11', padId, pad.head - 1, authorId);
      assert.equal(pad.head, oldHead + 1);
      assert.equal(await pad.getRevisionAuthor(pad.head), '');
    });
  });

  describe('v1.3.0', function () {
    it('change is attributed to given authorId', async function () {
      const oldHead = pad.head;
      await restoreRevision('1.3.0', padId, pad.head - 1, authorId);
      assert.equal(pad.head, oldHead + 1);
      assert.equal(await pad.getRevisionAuthor(pad.head), authorId);
    });

    it('authorId can be omitted', async function () {
      const oldHead = pad.head;
      await restoreRevision('1.3.0', padId, pad.head - 1);
      assert.equal(pad.head, oldHead + 1);
      assert.equal(await pad.getRevisionAuthor(pad.head), '');
    });
  });
});

Now I notice something: `getRevision` in PadType expects `(rev?: string)=>Promise<any>`, but it's called with a number in `_getChangesetsInBulk`. Let me check the actual Pad implementation more carefully.

But wait, the error is "Not a changeset: undefined". Let me think about what is being passed as `undefined` to `unpack`.

Let me trace through `_createDiffAtext` again:

1. `_createClearStartAtext(this._fromRev)` - this calls `getInternalRevisionAText(rev)` then `_createClearAuthorship(rev)` then `applyToAText`.

In `getInternalRevisionAText`:
```javascript
async getInternalRevisionAText(targetRev: number) {
    const keyRev = this.getKeyRevisionNumber(targetRev);
    const headRev = this.getHeadRevisionNumber();
    if (targetRev > headRev) targetRev = headRev;
    const [keyAText, changesets] = await Promise.all([
      this._getKeyRevisionAText(keyRev),
      Promise.all(
          Stream.range(keyRev + 1, targetRev + 1).map(this.getRevisionChangeset.bind(this))),
    ]);
    const apool = this.apool();
    let atext = keyAText;
    for (const cs of changesets) atext = applyToAText(cs, atext, apool);
    return atext;
  }
```

If `targetRev` is e.g., 1, and `keyRev` is 0, then `Stream.range(1, 2)` returns `[1]` and it calls `getRevisionChangeset(1)`. That should return a changeset string.

But what if `targetRev` is 0? Then `keyRev = 0`, `Stream.range(1, 1)` returns `[]`, and it just returns `keyAText`. That should work.

Now in `_createClearAuthorship`:
```javascript
async _createClearAuthorship(rev: any){
    const atext = await this._pad.getInternalRevisionAText(rev);
    const builder = new Builder(atext.text.length);
    builder.keepText(atext.text, [['author', '']], this._pad.pool);
    const changeset = builder.toString();
    return changeset;
  }
```

This should always return a valid changeset.

Then `_createClearStartAtext`:
```javascript
async _createClearStartAtext(rev: any){
    const atext = await this._pad.getInternalRevisionAText(rev);
    const changeset = await this._createClearAuthorship(rev);
    const newAText = applyToAText(changeset, atext, this._pad.pool);
    return newAText;
  }
```

Wait! `rev` is of type `any`. It could be a string or number. But looking at `getValidRevisionRange`, it returns `{startRev, endRev}` where both are numbers (from `parseInt`).

But wait, look at PadDiff constructor:
```javascript
this._fromRev = range.startRev;
this._toRev = range.endRev;
```

And `getValidRevisionRange`:
```javascript
getValidRevisionRange(startRev: any, endRev:any) {
    startRev = parseInt(startRev, 10);
    const head = this.getHeadRevisionNumber();
    endRev = endRev ? parseInt(endRev, 10) : head;
    ...
    if (startRev != null && endRev != null) {
      return {startRev, endRev};
    }
    return null;
  }
```

The issue is `endRev = endRev ? parseInt(endRev, 10) : head;`. If `endRev` is 0, this evaluates `0 ? ... : head` which is `head` (since 0 is falsy). So `endRev=0` becomes `head`. But that's a different issue.

But wait, the PR description says the issue happens with `startRev=1, endRev=1`. So both are truthy.

Now let me check `_getChangesetsInBulk` again more carefully:

```javascript
async _getChangesetsInBulk(startRev: any, count: any) {
    const revisions = [];
    for (let i = startRev; i < (startRev + count) && i <= this._pad.head; i++) {
      revisions.push(i);
    }

    const changesets:any[] = [];
    const authors: any[] = [];
    await Promise.all(revisions.map((rev) => this._pad.getRevision(rev).then((revision) => {
      const arrayNum = rev - startRev;
      changesets[arrayNum] = revision.changeset;
      authors[arrayNum] = revision.meta.author;
    })));

    return {changesets, authors};
  }
```

And in `_createDiffAtext`:
```javascript
for (let rev = this._fromRev + 1; rev <= this._toRev; rev += bulkSize) {
    const {changesets, authors} = await this._getChangesetsInBulk(rev, bulkSize);
    ...
    for (let i = 0; i < changesets.length && (rev + i) <= this._toRev; ++i) {
        let changeset = changesets[i];
        if (this._isClearAuthorship(changeset)) {
            continue;
        }
        changeset = this._extendChangesetWithAuthor(changeset, authors[i], this._pad.pool);
        ...
    }
}
```

Wait! Look at the inner loop condition: `for (let i = 0; i < changesets.length && (rev + i) <= this._toRev; ++i)`

If `this._fromRev = 1` and `this._toRev = 2`, the outer loop runs for `rev = 2`. Then `_getChangesetsInBulk(2, 100)` gets revisions [2, 3, ..., up to head]. The inner loop processes `changesets[0]` (which is rev 2) and then checks if `rev + i <= this._toRev` i.e., `2 + 1 <= 2` which is false, so only one revision is processed. That seems correct.

But what if `_getChangesetsInBulk` returns `changesets` with `undefined` entries? If a revision doesn't exist in the database, `getRevision` might return `undefined`, and then `revision.changeset` would fail... but the error would be different.

Actually wait. Let me re-read the error more carefully:

THERE IT IS! The prototype override at the bottom of the file replaces `_createDeletionChangeset` with an EMPTY FUNCTION that returns `undefined`. 

But wait, that's a prototype method. In the class definition, `_createDeletionChangeset` is defined as a method on the class. When you define methods in a class, they are defined on the prototype. So the prototype override would replace the class method with an empty function.

So the bug is that line 464-466 replaces the `_createDeletionChangeset` method with an empty function. This was probably left over from development/debugging.

But if startRev == endRev, `superChangeset` would be null and `_createDeletionChangeset` would not be called. So the error would not be at line 182. Unless there's another issue.

Let me look at the error again more carefully. The stack trace shows:
- `at unpack (/run/etherpad-lite/src/static/js/Changeset.ts:363:44)`
- `at error (/run/etherpad-lite/src/static/js/Changeset.ts:64:13)`
- ...

- `at applyToText (/run/etherpad-lite/src/static/js/Changeset.ts:405:20)`

The call chain is: `_createDiffAtext` at line 182 calls `applyToAText` which calls `applyToText` which calls `unpack` which calls `error`. So line 182 is the direct cause.

But for startRev == endRev, line 182 should NOT be reached. Unless there's another bug that causes the loop to execute even when startRev == endRev.

Wait, let me check the loop condition again:

```javascript
for (let rev = this._fromRev + 1; rev <= this._toRev; rev += bulkSize) {
```

If `_fromRev = 1` and `_toRev = 1`, then `rev = 2`, and `2 <= 1` is false, so the loop doesn't execute. That's correct.

But what about `_fromRev = 1` and `_toRev = 2`? Then `rev = 2`, `2 <= 2` is true, loop executes once. Then `_getChangesetsInBulk(2, 100)` gets revisions starting from 2. Then the inner loop processes changesets. If there's at least one non-clearAuthorship changeset, `superChangeset` gets set, and then `_createDeletionChangeset` is called and returns `undefined`. So the error occurs.

So the main fix is to remove the empty prototype override. But I should also check if there are other issues.

   283	        curLineOps = deserializeOps(aLinesGet(curLine));
   284	        curLineOpsNext = curLineOps!.next();
   285	        curLineOpsLine = curLine;

   289	          curLineOpsNext = curLineOps!.next();

   293	          }

   295	        }
   296	      }
   297

   301	          curChar = 0;
   302	          curLineOpsLine = curLine;

   305	          curLineOpsNext = curLineOps!.next();

   312	            curLineNextOp = curLineOpsNext!.value;
   313	            curLineOpsNext = curLineOps!.next();

   321	        numChars -= charsToUse;
   322	        curLineNextOp.chars -= charsToUse;

   324	      }
   325

   328	        curChar = 0;
   329	      }

   335	        curChar = 0;

   337	        consumeAttribRuns(N, () => {});

   346	      const firstString = linesGet(curLine).substring(curChar);
   347	      len += firstString.length;
   348	      assem.append(firstString);
   349	
   350	      let lineNum = curLine + 1;
   351	
   352	      while (len < numChars) {
   353	        const nextString = linesGet(lineNum);
   354	        len += nextString.length;
   355	        assem.append(nextString);
   356	        lineNum++;
   357	      }
   358	
   359	      return assem.toString().substring(0, numChars);
   360	    };

   362	    const cachedStrFunc = (func:Function) => {
   363	      const cache:MapArrayType<any> = {};
   364	
   365	      return (s:string) => {
   366	        if (!cache[s]) {
   367	          cache[s] = func(s);
   368	        }
   369	        return cache[s];
   370	      };
   371	    };
   372

   374	      if (csOp.opcode === '=') {
   375	        const textBank = nextText(csOp.chars);
   376	
   377	        // decide if this equal operator is an attribution change or not.
   378	        // We can see this by checkinf if attribs is set.
   379	        // If the text this operator applies to is only a star,

   381	        if (csOp.attribs && textBank !== '*') {
   382	          const attribs = AttributeMap.fromString(csOp.attribs, apool);
   383	          const undoBackToAttribs = cachedStrFunc((oldAttribsStr: string) => {
   384	            const oldAttribs = AttributeMap.fromString(oldAttribsStr, apool);
   385	            const backAttribs = new AttributeMap(apool)
   386	                .set('author', '')
   387	                .set('removed', 'true');
   388	            for (const [key, value] of attribs) {
   389	              const oldValue = oldAttribs.get(key);
   390	              if (oldValue !== value) backAttribs.set(key, oldValue);
   391	            }

   395	          });
   396	
   397	          let textLeftToProcess = textBank;
   398	
   399	          while (textLeftToProcess.length > 0) {
   400	            // process till the next line break or process only one line break
   401	            let lengthToProcess = textLeftToProcess.indexOf('\n');
   402	            let lineBreak = false;
   403	            switch (lengthToProcess) {
   404	              case -1:
   405	                lengthToProcess = textLeftToProcess.length;
   406	                break;
   407	              case 0:
   408	                lineBreak = true;
   409	                lengthToProcess = 1;
   410	                break;

   417	
   418	            if (lineBreak) {
   419	              builder.keep(1, 1); // just skip linebreaks, don't do a insert + keep for a linebreak
   420	
   421	              // consume the attributes of this linebreak
   422	              consumeAttribRuns(1, () => {});
   423	            } else {
   424	              // add the old text via an insert, but add a deletion attribute +
   425	              // the author attribute of the author who deleted it
   426	              let textBankIndex = 0;
   427	              consumeAttribRuns(lengthToProcess, (len: number, attribs:string, endsLine: string) => {
   428	                // get the old attributes back
   429	                const oldAttribs = undoBackToAttribs(attribs);
   430	
   431	                builder.insert(processText.substr(textBankIndex, len), oldAttribs);
   432	                textBankIndex += len;
   433	              });
   434	
   435	              builder.keep(lengthToProcess, 0);
   436	            }
   437	          }
   438	        } else {
   439	          skip(csOp.chars, csOp.lines);
   440	          builder.keep(csOp.chars, csOp.lines);
   441	        }
   442	      } else if (csOp.opcode === '+') {
   443	        builder.keep(csOp.chars, csOp.lines);
   444	      } else if (csOp.opcode === '-') {
   445	        const textBank = nextText(csOp.chars);
   446	        let textBankIndex = 0;
   447	
   448	        consumeAttribRuns(csOp.chars, (len: number, attribs: string[], endsLine: string) => {
   449	          builder.insert(textBank.substr(textBankIndex, len), attribs + csOp.attribs);
   450	          textBankIndex += len;
   451	        });
   452	      }
   453	    }

    50	  /**
    51	   * @param id

    53	   *     the shared global Etherpad database object is still used for all other pad accesses, such
    54	   *     as copying the pad). Defaults to the shared global Etherpad database object. This parameter

    58	  constructor(id:string, database = db) {

    60	    this.atext = makeAText('\n');
    61	    this.pool = new AttributePool();
    62	    this.head = -1;
    63	    this.chatHead = -1;
    64	    this.publicStatus = false;
    65	    this.id = id;
    66	    this.savedRevisions = [];
    67	  }
    68	
    69	  apool() {
    70	    return this.pool;
    71	  }
    72	
    73	  getHeadRevisionNumber() {
    74	    return this.head;
    75	  }
    76	
    77	  getSavedRevisionsNumber() {
    78	    return this.savedRevisions.length;
    79	  }
    80	
    81	  getSavedRevisionsList() {
    82	    const savedRev = this.savedRevisions.map((rev) => rev.revNum);
    83	    savedRev.sort((a, b) => a - b);
    84	    return savedRev;
    85	  }
    86	
    87	  getPublicStatus() {
    88	    return this.publicStatus;
    89	  }
    90	
    91	  /**
    92	   * Appends a new revision
    93	   * @param {Object} aChangeset The changeset to append to the pad
    94	   * @param {String} authorId The id of the author
    95	   * @return {Promise<number|string>}
    96	   */
    97	  async appendRevision(aChangeset:string, authorId = '') {
    98	    const newAText = applyToAText(aChangeset, this.atext, this.pool);
    99	    if (newAText.text === this.atext.text && newAText.attribs === this.atext.attribs &&
   100	        this.head !== -1) {
   101	      return this.head;
   102	    }
   103	    copyAText(newAText, this.atext);
   104	
   105	    const newRev = ++this.head;
   106	
   107	    // ex. getNumForAuthor
   108	    if (authorId !== '') this.pool.putAttrib(['author', authorId]);
   109	
   110	    const hook = this.head === 0 ? 'padCreate' : 'padUpdate';
   111	    await Promise.all([
   112	      // @ts-ignore
   113	      this.db.set(`pad:${this.id}:revs:${newRev}`, {
   114	        changeset: aChangeset,
   115	        meta: {
   116	          author: authorId,
   117	          timestamp: Date.now(),
   118	          ...newRev === this.getKeyRevisionNumber(newRev) ? {
   119	            pool: this.pool,
   120	            atext: this.atext,

So `head` starts at -1 and after the first revision becomes 0. So revision 0 IS valid. But the `getValidRevisionRange` has a bug: if `endRev` is 0, it gets replaced by `head`.

Also `startRev` could be 0, but `parseInt(startRev, 10)` would be 0, which is valid. But then `if (isNaN(startRev) || startRev < 0 || startRev > head)` - 0 < 0 is false, so startRev=0 is fine as long as head >= 0.

But the main issue is the prototype override. Let me also fix the `getValidRevisionRange` bug with `endRev = 0`.

   831	  // check if startRev is a number
   832	  if (startRev !== undefined) {
   833	    startRev = checkValidRev(startRev);
   834	  }
   835	
   836	  // check if endRev is a number

     3	const CustomError = require('../utils/customError');
     4	
     5	// checks if a rev is a legal number
     6	// pre-condition is that `rev` is not undefined
     7	const checkValidRev = (rev: number|string) => {
     8	  if (typeof rev !== 'number') {
     9	    rev = parseInt(rev, 10);
    10	  }
    11	
    12	  // check if rev is a number
    13	  if (isNaN(rev)) {
    14	    throw new CustomError('rev is not a number', 'apierror');
    15	  }
    16	
    17	  // ensure this is not a negative number
    18	  if (rev < 0) {
    19	    throw new CustomError('rev is not a negative number', 'apierror');
    20	  }
    21	
    22	  // ensure this is not a float value
    23	  if (!isInt(rev)) {
    24	    throw new CustomError('rev is a float value', 'apierror');
    25	  }
    26	
    27	  return rev;
    28	};

    30	// checks if a number is an int
    31	const isInt = (value:number) => (parseFloat(String(value)) === parseInt(String(value), 10)) && !isNaN(value);

The prototype now has the proper class method instead of the empty override.

Now let me think about edge cases:

1. **`startRev === endRev`**: The loop in `_createDiffAtext` won't execute, `superChangeset` stays null, and the function returns the clear start atext. This should work correctly now since we're not calling `_createDeletionChangeset`.

2. **`endRev = 0`**: With my fix to `getValidRevisionRange`, `endRev = 0` is now properly handled (not treated as falsy).

3. **`startRev = 0`**: Already worked before (parseInt gives 0, which is valid).

4. **Only clearAuthorship changesets**: If all changesets between `_fromRev+1` and `_toRev` are clearAuthorship, `superChangeset` stays null, and `_createDeletionChangeset` is never called. This is fine.

But wait, I should also double-check another potential issue. Let me look at `_getChangesetsInBulk` again:

```javascript
async _getChangesetsInBulk(startRev: any, count: any) {
    const revisions = [];
    for (let i = startRev; i < (startRev + count) && i <= this._pad.head; i++) {
      revisions.push(i);
    }
    ...
}
```

If `startRev` is 1 and `this._pad.head` is 0 (pad with only initial revision), the loop doesn't execute, `revisions` is empty, and the function returns `{changesets: [], authors: []}`. Then in `_createDiffAtext`, the inner loop `for (let i = 0; i < changesets.length && ...)` doesn't execute, `superChangeset` stays null. This is fine.

But what about the case where `_fromRev` equals `head`? Then the outer loop starts at `head + 1` which is > `_toRev` (which is head), so the loop doesn't execute. This is fine.

Let me also check if there are potential issues with `_extendChangesetWithAuthor`. Looking at line 215-241:

```javascript
_extendChangesetWithAuthor(changeset: any, author: any, apool: any){
    const unpacked = unpack(changeset);
    const assem = new OpAssembler();
    const authorAttrib = apool.putAttrib(['author', author || '']);
    const deletedAttrib = apool.putAttrib(['removed', true]);
    const attribs = `*${numToString(authorAttrib)}*${numToString(deletedAttrib)}`;
    for (const operator of deserializeOps(unpacked.ops)) {
      if (operator.opcode === '-') {
        operator.attribs = attribs;
      } else if (operator.opcode === '=' && operator.attribs) {
        operator.attribs += `*${numToString(authorAttrib)}`;
      }
      assem.append(operator);
    }
    return pack(unpacked.oldLen, unpacked.newLen, assem.toString(), unpacked.charBank);
  }
```

This calls `unpack(changeset)` which would also throw "Not a changeset: undefined" if `changeset` is undefined. But `changeset` comes from `changesets[i]` in `_createDiffAtext`, which comes from `_getChangesetsInBulk`. If a revision doesn't exist, `getRevision` might return something unexpected. But that's a separate issue from the one reported.
