     2	 * Incrementally builds a Changeset.
     3	 *
     4	 * @typedef {object} Builder
     5	 * @property {Function} insert -
     6	 * @property {Function} keep -
     7	 * @property {Function} keepText -
     8	 * @property {Function} remove -
     9	 * @property {Function} toString -
    10	 */
    11	import {SmartOpAssembler} from "./SmartOpAssembler";
    12	import Op from "./Op";
    13	import {StringAssembler} from "./StringAssembler";
    14	import AttributeMap from "./AttributeMap";
    15	import {Attribute} from "./types/Attribute";
    16	import AttributePool from "./AttributePool";
    17	import {opsFromText, pack} from "./Changeset";
    18	
    19	/**
    20	 * @param {number} oldLen - Old length
    21	 * @returns {Builder}
    22	 */
    23	export class Builder {
    24	  private readonly oldLen: number;
    25	  private assem: SmartOpAssembler;
    26	  private readonly o: Op;
    27	  private charBank: StringAssembler;
    28	
    29	  constructor(oldLen: number) {
    30	    this.oldLen = oldLen
    31	    this.assem = new SmartOpAssembler()
    32	    this.o = new Op()
    33	    this.charBank = new StringAssembler()
    34	  }
    35	
    36	  /**
    37	   * @param {number} N - Number of characters to keep.
    38	   * @param {number} L - Number of newlines among the `N` characters. If positive, the last
    39	   *     character must be a newline.
    40	   * @param {(string|Attribute[])} attribs - Either [[key1,value1],[key2,value2],...] or '*0*1...'
    41	   *     (no pool needed in latter case).
    42	   * @param {?AttributePool.ts} pool - Attribute pool, only required if `attribs` is a list of
    43	   *     attribute key, value pairs.
    44	   * @returns {Builder} this
    45	   */
    46	  keep =  (N: number, L?: number, attribs?: string|Attribute[], pool?: AttributePool): Builder => {
    47	    this.o.opcode = '=';
    48	    this.o.attribs = typeof attribs === 'string'
    49	      ? attribs : new AttributeMap(pool).update(attribs || []).toString();
    50	    this.o.chars = N;
    51	    this.o.lines = (L || 0);
    52	    this.assem.append(this.o);
    53	    return this;
    54	  }
    55	
    56	
    57	  /**
    58	   * @param {string} text - Text to keep.
    59	   * @param {(string|Attribute[])} attribs - Either [[key1,value1],[key2,value2],...] or '*0*1...'
    60	   *     (no pool needed in latter case).
    61	   * @param {?AttributePool.ts} pool - Attribute pool, only required if `attribs` is a list of
    62	   *     attribute key, value pairs.
    63	   * @returns {Builder} this
    64	   */
    65	  keepText= (text: string, attribs?: string|Attribute[], pool?: AttributePool): Builder=> {
    66	    for (const op of opsFromText('=', text, attribs, pool)) this.assem.append(op);
    67	    return this;
    68	  }
    69	
    70	
    71	  /**
    72	   * @param {string} text - Text to insert.
    73	   * @param {(string|Attribute[])} attribs - Either [[key1,value1],[key2,value2],...] or '*0*1...'
    74	   *     (no pool needed in latter case).
    75	   * @param {?AttributePool.ts} pool - Attribute pool, only required if `attribs` is a list of
    76	   *     attribute key, value pairs.
    77	   * @returns {Builder} this
    78	   */
    79	  insert= (text: string, attribs: string | Attribute[] | undefined, pool?: AttributePool | null | undefined): Builder => {
    80	    for (const op of opsFromText('+', text, attribs, pool)) this.assem.append(op);
    81	    this.charBank.append(text);
    82	    return this;
    83	  }
    84	
    85	
    86	  /**
    87	   * @param {number} N - Number of characters to remove.
    88	   * @param {number} L - Number of newlines among the `N` characters. If positive, the last
    89	   *     character must be a newline.
    90	   * @returns {Builder} this
    91	   */
    92	  remove= (N: number, L?: number): Builder => {
    93	    this.o.opcode = '-';
    94	    this.o.attribs = '';
    95	    this.o.chars = N;
    96	    this.o.lines = (L || 0);
    97	    this.assem.append(this.o);
    98	    return this;
    99	  }
   100	
   101	  toString= () => {
   102	    this.assem.endDocument();
   103	    const newLen = this.oldLen + this.assem.getLengthChange();
   104	    return pack(this.oldLen, newLen, this.assem.toString(), this.charBank.toString());
   105	  }
   106	}
   107	
   108	
   109

Now let me also check if there's any issue with the `_createClearStartAtext` when the revision is 0. Let me trace through:

1. `_createClearStartAtext(0)` calls `_pad.getInternalRevisionAText(0)`
2. `getInternalRevisionAText(0)`:
   - `keyRev = getKeyRevisionNumber(0) = 0`
   - `headRev = getHeadRevisionNumber()` 
   - `targetRev = 0` (if `targetRev > headRev` it would be clamped, but for a pad with edits, head >= 0)
   - `[keyAText, changesets] = await Promise.all([this._getKeyRevisionAText(0), Promise.all(Stream.range(1, 1).map(...))])`
   - `Stream.range(1, 1)` returns `[]`, so `changesets` is `[]`
   - Returns `keyAText`

This should work.

But wait, what about revision 0's key atext? Let me check `_getKeyRevisionAText`:

```javascript
async _getKeyRevisionAText(revNum: number) {
    return await this.db.getSub(`pad:${this.id}:revs:${revNum}`, ['meta', 'atext']);
}
```

For a newly created pad, revision 0 is the first "real" revision (the pad starts with `head = -1`, and after the first edit, `head = 0`). But `_getKeyRevisionAText(0)` would look for `pad:${id}:revs:0`. Does this exist?

Looking at `appendRevision`:
```javascript
const newRev = ++this.head; // For first edit: head goes from -1 to 0
// ...
this.db.set(`pad:${this.id}:revs:${newRev}`, {
    changeset: aChangeset,
    meta: {
        author: authorId,
        timestamp: Date.now(),
        ...newRev === this.getKeyRevisionNumber(newRev) ? {
            pool: this.pool,
            atext: this.atext,
        } : {},
    },
});
```

So for rev 0, `getKeyRevisionNumber(0) = 0`, and `newRev === 0`, so it stores `atext` in the meta. So `_getKeyRevisionAText(0)` should work.

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /testbed/src/node/utils/padDiff.ts:
     1	'use strict';
     2	
     3	import {PadAuthor, PadType} from "../types/PadType";
     4	import {MapArrayType} from "../types/MapType";
     5	
     6	import AttributeMap from '../../static/js/AttributeMap';
     7	import {applyToAText, checkRep, compose, deserializeOps, pack, splitAttributionLines, splitTextLines, unpack} from '../../static/js/Changeset';
     8	import {Builder} from "../../static/js/Builder";
     9	import {OpAssembler} from "../../static/js/OpAssembler";
    10	import {numToString} from "../../static/js/ChangesetUtils";
    11	import Op from "../../static/js/Op";
    12	import {StringAssembler} from "../../static/js/StringAssembler";
    13	const attributes = require('../../static/js/attributes');
    14	const exportHtml = require('./ExportHtml');
    15	
    16	
    17	class PadDiff {
    18	  private readonly _pad: PadType;
    19	    private readonly _fromRev: string;
    20	    private readonly _toRev: string;
    21	    private _html: any;
    22	    public _authors: any[];
    23	    private self: PadDiff | undefined
    24	  constructor(pad: PadType, fromRev:string, toRev:string) {
    25	    // check parameters
    26	    if (!pad || !pad.id || !pad.atext || !pad.pool) {
    27	      throw new Error('Invalid pad');
    28	    }
    29	
    30	    const range = pad.getValidRevisionRange(fromRev, toRev);
    31	    if (!range) throw new Error(`Invalid revision range. startRev: ${fromRev} endRev: ${toRev}`);
    32	
    33	    this._pad = pad;
    34	    this._fromRev = range.startRev;
    35	    this._toRev = range.endRev;
    36	    this._html = null;
    37	    this._authors = [];
    38	  }
    39	  _isClearAuthorship(changeset: any){
    40	    // unpack
    41	    const unpacked = unpack(changeset);
    42	
    43	    // check if there is nothing in the charBank
    44	    if (unpacked.charBank !== '') {
    45	      return false;
    46	    }
    47	
    48	    // check if oldLength == newLength
    49	    if (unpacked.oldLen !== unpacked.newLen) {
    50	      return false;
    51	    }
    52	
    53	    const [clearOperator, anotherOp] = deserializeOps(unpacked.ops);
    54	
    55	    // check if there is only one operator
    56	    if (anotherOp != null) return false;
    57	
    58	    // check if this operator doesn't change text
    59	    if (clearOperator.opcode !== '=') {
    60	      return false;
    61	    }
    62	
    63	    // check that this operator applys to the complete text
    64	    // if the text ends with a new line, its exactly one character less, else it has the same length
    65	    if (clearOperator.chars !== unpacked.oldLen - 1 && clearOperator.chars !== unpacked.oldLen) {
    66	      return false;
    67	    }
    68	
    69	    const [appliedAttribute, anotherAttribute] =
    70	        attributes.attribsFromString(clearOperator.attribs, this._pad.pool);
    71	
    72	    // Check that the operation has exactly one attribute.
    73	    if (appliedAttribute == null || anotherAttribute != null) return false;
    74	
    75	    // check if the applied attribute is an anonymous author attribute
    76	    if (appliedAttribute[0] !== 'author' || appliedAttribute[1] !== '') {
    77	      return false;
    78	    }
    79	
    80	    return true;
    81	  }
    82	  async _createClearAuthorship(rev: any){
    83	    const atext = await this._pad.getInternalRevisionAText(rev);
    84	
    85	    // build clearAuthorship changeset
    86	    const builder = new Builder(atext.text.length);
    87	    builder.keepText(atext.text, [['author', '']], this._pad.pool);
    88	    const changeset = builder.toString();
    89	
    90	    return changeset;
    91	  }
    92	
    93	  async _createClearStartAtext(rev: any){
    94	    // get the atext of this revision
    95	    const atext = await this._pad.getInternalRevisionAText(rev);
    96	
    97	    // create the clearAuthorship changeset
    98	    const changeset = await this._createClearAuthorship(rev);
    99	
   100	    // apply the clearAuthorship changeset
   101	    const newAText = applyToAText(changeset, atext, this._pad.pool);
   102	
   103	    return newAText;
   104	  }
   105	  async _getChangesetsInBulk(startRev: any, count: any) {
   106	    // find out which revisions we need
   107	    const revisions = [];
   108	    for (let i = startRev; i < (startRev + count) && i <= this._pad.head; i++) {
   109	      revisions.push(i);
   110	    }
   111	
   112	    // get all needed revisions (in parallel)
   113	    const changesets:any[] = [];
   114	    const authors: any[] = [];
   115	    await Promise.all(revisions.map((rev) => this._pad.getRevision(rev).then((revision) => {
   116	      const arrayNum = rev - startRev;
   117	      changesets[arrayNum] = revision.changeset;
   118	      authors[arrayNum] = revision.meta.author;
   119	    })));
   120	
   121	    return {changesets, authors};
   122	  }
   123	  _addAuthors(authors: PadAuthor[]){
   124	      this.self = this;
   125	
   126	    // add to array if not in the array
   127	    authors.forEach((author) => {
   128	      if (this.self!._authors.indexOf(author) === -1) {
   129	        this.self!._authors.push(author);
   130	      }
   131	    });
   132	  }
   133	  async _createDiffAtext(){
   134	    const bulkSize = 100;
   135	
   136	    // get the cleaned startAText
   137	    let atext = await this._createClearStartAtext(this._fromRev);
   138	
   139	    let superChangeset = null;
   140	
   141	    for (let rev = this._fromRev + 1; rev <= this._toRev; rev += bulkSize) {
   142	      // get the bulk
   143	      const {changesets, authors} = await this._getChangesetsInBulk(rev, bulkSize);
   144	
   145	      const addedAuthors = [];
   146	
   147	      // run through all changesets
   148	      for (let i = 0; i < changesets.length && (rev + i) <= this._toRev; ++i) {
   149	        let changeset = changesets[i];
   150	
   151	        // skip clearAuthorship Changesets
   152	        if (this._isClearAuthorship(changeset)) {
   153	          continue;
   154	        }
   155	
   156	        changeset = this._extendChangesetWithAuthor(changeset, authors[i], this._pad.pool);
   157	
   158	        // add this author to the authorarray
   159	        addedAuthors.push(authors[i]);
   160	
   161	        // compose it with the superChangset
   162	        if (superChangeset == null) {
   163	          superChangeset = changeset;
   164	        } else {
   165	          superChangeset = compose(superChangeset, changeset, this._pad.pool);
   166	        }
   167	      }
   168	
   169	      // add the authors to the PadDiff authorArray
   170	      this._addAuthors(addedAuthors);
   171	    }
   172	
   173	    // if there are only clearAuthorship changesets, we don't get a superChangeset,
   174	    // so we can skip this step
   175	    if (superChangeset) {
   176	      const deletionChangeset = this._createDeletionChangeset(superChangeset, atext, this._pad.pool);
   177	
   178	      // apply the superChangeset, which includes all addings
   179	      atext = applyToAText(superChangeset, atext, this._pad.pool);
   180	
   181	      // apply the deletionChangeset, which adds a deletions
   182	      atext = applyToAText(deletionChangeset, atext, this._pad.pool);
   183	    }
   184	
   185	    return atext;
   186	  }
   187	  async getHtml(){
   188	    // cache the html
   189	    if (this._html != null) {
   190	      return this._html;
   191	    }
   192	
   193	    // get the diff atext
   194	    const atext = await this._createDiffAtext();
   195	
   196	    // get the authorColor table
   197	    const authorColors = await this._pad.getAllAuthorColors();
   198	
   199	    // convert the atext to html
   200	    this._html = await exportHtml.getHTMLFromAtext(this._pad, atext, authorColors);
   201	
   202	    return this._html;
   203	  }
   204	
   205	  async getAuthors() {
   206	    // check if html was already produced, if not produce it, this generates
   207	    // the author array at the same time
   208	    if (this._html == null) {
   209	      await this.getHtml();
   210	    }
   211	
   212	    return this.self!._authors;
   213	  }
   214	
   215	  _extendChangesetWithAuthor(changeset: any, author: any, apool: any){
   216	    // unpack
   217	    const unpacked = unpack(changeset);
   218	
   219	    const assem = new OpAssembler();
   220	
   221	    // create deleted attribs
   222	    const authorAttrib = apool.putAttrib(['author', author || '']);
   223	    const deletedAttrib = apool.putAttrib(['removed', true]);
   224	    const attribs = `*${numToString(authorAttrib)}*${numToString(deletedAttrib)}`;
   225	
   226	    for (const operator of deserializeOps(unpacked.ops)) {
   227	      if (operator.opcode === '-') {
   228	        // this is a delete operator, extend it with the author
   229	        operator.attribs = attribs;
   230	      } else if (operator.opcode === '=' && operator.attribs) {
   231	        // this is operator changes only attributes, let's mark which author did that
   232	        operator.attribs += `*${numToString(authorAttrib)}`;
   233	      }
   234	
   235	      // append the new operator to our assembler
   236	      assem.append(operator);
   237	    }
   238	
   239	    // return the modified changeset
   240	    return pack(unpacked.oldLen, unpacked.newLen, assem.toString(), unpacked.charBank);
   241	  }
   242	  _createDeletionChangeset(cs: any, startAText: any, apool: any){
   243	    const lines = splitTextLines(startAText.text);
   244	    const alines = splitAttributionLines(startAText.attribs, startAText.text);
   245	
   246	    // lines and alines are what the exports is meant to apply to.
   247	    // They may be arrays or objects with .get(i) and .length methods.
   248	    // They include final newlines on lines.
   249	
   250	    const linesGet = (idx: number) => {
   251	      // @ts-ignore
   252	      if (lines.get) {
   253	        // @ts-ignore
   254	        return lines.get(idx);
   255	      } else {
   256	        // @ts-ignore
   257	        return lines[idx];
   258	      }
   259	    };
   260	
   261	    const aLinesGet = (idx: number) => {
   262	      // @ts-ignore
   263	      if (alines.get) {
   264	        // @ts-ignore
   265	        return alines.get(idx);
   266	      } else {
   267	        return alines[idx];
   268	      }
   269	    };
   270	
   271	    let curLine = 0;
   272	    let curChar = 0;
   273	    let curLineOps: { next: () => any; } | null = null;
   274	    let curLineOpsNext: { done: any; value: any; } | null = null;
   275	    let curLineOpsLine: number;
   276	    let curLineNextOp = new Op('+');
   277	
   278	    const unpacked = unpack(cs);
   279	    const builder = new Builder(unpacked.newLen);
   280	
   281	    const consumeAttribRuns = (numChars: number, func: Function /* (len, attribs, endsLine)*/) => {
   282	      if (!curLineOps || curLineOpsLine !== curLine) {
   283	        curLineOps = deserializeOps(aLinesGet(curLine));
   284	        curLineOpsNext = curLineOps!.next();
   285	        curLineOpsLine = curLine;
   286	        let indexIntoLine = 0;
   287	        while (!curLineOpsNext!.done) {
   288	          curLineNextOp = curLineOpsNext!.value;
   289	          curLineOpsNext = curLineOps!.next();
   290	          if (indexIntoLine + curLineNextOp.chars >= curChar) {
   291	            curLineNextOp.chars -= (curChar - indexIntoLine);
   292	            break;
   293	          }
   294	          indexIntoLine += curLineNextOp.chars;
   295	        }
   296	      }
   297	
   298	      while (numChars > 0) {
   299	        if (!curLineNextOp.chars && curLineOpsNext!.done) {
   300	          curLine++;
   301	          curChar = 0;
   302	          curLineOpsLine = curLine;
   303	          curLineNextOp.chars = 0;
   304	          curLineOps = deserializeOps(aLinesGet(curLine));
   305	          curLineOpsNext = curLineOps!.next();
   306	        }
   307	
   308	        if (!curLineNextOp.chars) {
   309	          if (curLineOpsNext!.done) {
   310	            curLineNextOp = new Op();
   311	          } else {
   312	            curLineNextOp = curLineOpsNext!.value;
   313	            curLineOpsNext = curLineOps!.next();
   314	          }
   315	        }
   316	
   317	        const charsToUse = Math.min(numChars, curLineNextOp.chars);
   318	
   319	        func(charsToUse, curLineNextOp.attribs,
   320	            charsToUse === curLineNextOp.chars && curLineNextOp.lines > 0);
   321	        numChars -= charsToUse;
   322	        curLineNextOp.chars -= charsToUse;
   323	        curChar += charsToUse;
   324	      }
   325	
   326	      if (!curLineNextOp.chars && curLineOpsNext!.done) {
   327	        curLine++;
   328	        curChar = 0;
   329	      }
   330	    };
   331	
   332	    const skip = (N:number, L:number) => {
   333	      if (L) {
   334	        curLine += L;
   335	        curChar = 0;
   336	      } else if (curLineOps && curLineOpsLine === curLine) {
   337	        consumeAttribRuns(N, () => {});
   338	      } else {
   339	        curChar += N;
   340	      }
   341	    };
   342	
   343	    const nextText = (numChars: number) => {
   344	      let len = 0;
   345	      const assem = new StringAssembler();
   346	      const firstString = linesGet(curLine).substring(curChar);
   347	      len += firstString.length;
   348	      assem.append(firstString);
   349	
   350	      let lineNum = curLine + 1;
   351	
   352	      while (len < numChars) {
   353	        const nextString = linesGet(lineNum);
   354	        len += nextString.length;
   355	        assem.append(nextString);
   356	        lineNum++;
   357	      }
   358	
   359	      return assem.toString().substring(0, numChars);
   360	    };
   361	
   362	    const cachedStrFunc = (func:Function) => {
   363	      const cache:MapArrayType<any> = {};
   364	
   365	      return (s:string) => {
   366	        if (!cache[s]) {
   367	          cache[s] = func(s);
   368	        }
   369	        return cache[s];
   370	      };
   371	    };
   372	
   373	    for (const csOp of deserializeOps(unpacked.ops)) {
   374	      if (csOp.opcode === '=') {
   375	        const textBank = nextText(csOp.chars);
   376	
   377	        // decide if this equal operator is an attribution change or not.
   378	        // We can see this by checkinf if attribs is set.
   379	        // If the text this operator applies to is only a star,
   380	        // than this is a false positive and should be ignored
   381	        if (csOp.attribs && textBank !== '*') {
   382	          const attribs = AttributeMap.fromString(csOp.attribs, apool);
   383	          const undoBackToAttribs = cachedStrFunc((oldAttribsStr: string) => {
   384	            const oldAttribs = AttributeMap.fromString(oldAttribsStr, apool);
   385	            const backAttribs = new AttributeMap(apool)
   386	                .set('author', '')
   387	                .set('removed', 'true');
   388	            for (const [key, value] of attribs) {
   389	              const oldValue = oldAttribs.get(key);
   390	              if (oldValue !== value) backAttribs.set(key, oldValue);
   391	            }
   392	            // TODO: backAttribs does not restore removed attributes (it is missing attributes that
   393	            // are in oldAttribs but not in attribs). I don't know if that is intentional.
   394	            return backAttribs.toString();
   395	          });
   396	
   397	          let textLeftToProcess = textBank;
   398	
   399	          while (textLeftToProcess.length > 0) {
   400	            // process till the next line break or process only one line break
   401	            let lengthToProcess = textLeftToProcess.indexOf('\n');
   402	            let lineBreak = false;
   403	            switch (lengthToProcess) {
   404	              case -1:
   405	                lengthToProcess = textLeftToProcess.length;
   406	                break;
   407	              case 0:
   408	                lineBreak = true;
   409	                lengthToProcess = 1;
   410	                break;
   411	            }
   412	
   413	            // get the text we want to procceed in this step
   414	            const processText = textLeftToProcess.substr(0, lengthToProcess);
   415	
   416	            textLeftToProcess = textLeftToProcess.substr(lengthToProcess);
   417	
   418	            if (lineBreak) {
   419	              builder.keep(1, 1); // just skip linebreaks, don't do a insert + keep for a linebreak
   420	
   421	              // consume the attributes of this linebreak
   422	              consumeAttribRuns(1, () => {});
   423	            } else {
   424	              // add the old text via an insert, but add a deletion attribute +
   425	              // the author attribute of the author who deleted it
   426	              let textBankIndex = 0;
   427	              consumeAttribRuns(lengthToProcess, (len: number, attribs:string, endsLine: string) => {
   428	                // get the old attributes back
   429	                const oldAttribs = undoBackToAttribs(attribs);
   430	
   431	                builder.insert(processText.substr(textBankIndex, len), oldAttribs);
   432	                textBankIndex += len;
   433	              });
   434	
   435	              builder.keep(lengthToProcess, 0);
   436	            }
   437	          }
   438	        } else {
   439	          skip(csOp.chars, csOp.lines);
   440	          builder.keep(csOp.chars, csOp.lines);
   441	        }
   442	      } else if (csOp.opcode === '+') {
   443	        builder.keep(csOp.chars, csOp.lines);
   444	      } else if (csOp.opcode === '-') {
   445	        const textBank = nextText(csOp.chars);
   446	        let textBankIndex = 0;
   447	
   448	        consumeAttribRuns(csOp.chars, (len: number, attribs: string[], endsLine: string) => {
   449	          builder.insert(textBank.substr(textBankIndex, len), attribs + csOp.attribs);
   450	          textBankIndex += len;
   451	        });
   452	      }
   453	    }
   454	
   455	    return checkRep(builder.toString());
   456	  }
   457	
   458	}
   459	
   460	
   461	// export the constructor
   462	module.exports = PadDiff;
   463

2026-05-30 08:12:30,909 - DEBUG - swea-lm-ether__etherpad-7445 - total_tokens_sent=1,767,796, total_tokens_received=8,668, total_cost=0.00, total_api_calls=51
2026-05-30 08:12:30,911 - INFO - swea-agent-ether__etherpad-7445 - 💭 THOUGHT
TypeScript compilation passes without errors.

Let me now summarize the changes I made:

1. **Main fix: Removed the empty prototype override of `_createDeletionChangeset` in `/testbed/src/node/utils/padDiff.ts`** (lines 461-466)

   The original code had an empty function assigned to `PadDiff.prototype._createDeletionChangeset` which overrode the actual implementation of the method in the class. This caused `_createDeletionChangeset` to return `undefined` instead of a valid changeset string. When `applyToAText` tried to call `unpack(undefined)`, it threw `"Not a changeset: undefined"`.

2. **Secondary fix: Fixed the falsy check for `endRev` in `getValidRevisionRange` in `/testbed/src/node/db/Pad.ts`** (line 244)

   The original code `endRev = endRev ? parseInt(endRev, 10) : head` would treat `endRev = 0` as falsy and replace it with `head`. Changed to `endRev = (endRev != null) ? parseInt(endRev, 10) : head` to properly handle the case where `endRev` is `0` (a valid revision number).

Both changes are minimal and directly address the issues described in the PR.

+      basic behavior
+        ✔ calls all asynchronously, returns values in order
+        ✔ passes hook name
+        ✔ undefined context -> {}
+        ✔ null context -> {}
+        ✔ context unmodified
+      aCallAll callback
+        ✔ exception in callback rejects
+        ✔ propagates error on exception
+        ✔ propagages null error on success
+        ✔ propagages results on success
+        ✔ returns callback return value
+      result processing
+        ✔ no registered hooks (undefined) -> []
+        ✔ no registered hooks (empty list) -> []
+        ✔ flattens one level
+        ✔ filters out undefined
+        ✔ preserves null
+        ✔ all undefined -> []
+    hooks.callAllSerial
+      basic behavior
+        ✔ calls all asynchronously, serially, in order
+        ✔ passes hook name
+        ✔ undefined context -> {}
+        ✔ null context -> {}
+        ✔ context unmodified
+      result processing
+        ✔ no registered hooks (undefined) -> []
+        ✔ no registered hooks (empty list) -> []
+        ✔ flattens one level
+        ✔ filters out undefined
+        ✔ preserves null
+        ✔ all undefined -> []
+    hooks.aCallFirst
+      ✔ no registered hooks (undefined) -> []
+      ✔ no registered hooks (empty list) -> []
+      ✔ passes hook name => {}
+      ✔ undefined context => {}
+      ✔ null context => {}
+      ✔ context unmodified
+      ✔ default predicate: predicate never satisfied -> calls all in order
+      ✔ calls hook functions serially
+      ✔ default predicate: stops when satisfied
+      ✔ default predicate: skips values that do not satisfy (undefined)
+      ✔ default predicate: skips values that do not satisfy (empty list)
+      ✔ default predicate: null satisifes
+      ✔ custom predicate: called for each hook function
+      ✔ custom predicate: boolean false/true continues/stops iteration
+      ✔ custom predicate: non-boolean falsy/truthy continues/stops iteration
+      ✔ custom predicate: array value passed unmodified to predicate
+      ✔ custom predicate: normalized value passed to predicate (undefined)
+      ✔ custom predicate: normalized value passed to predicate (null)
+      ✔ non-empty arrays are returned unmodified
+      ✔ value can be passed via callback

+        ✔ immediately call cb(value) then defer call to cb(value) (diff. outcomes) -> log+throw

+    hooks.callAll
+      basic behavior
+        ✔ calls all in order
+        ✔ passes hook name
+        ✔ undefined context -> {}
+        ✔ null context -> {}
+        ✔ context unmodified
+      result processing
+        ✔ no registered hooks (undefined) -> []
+        ✔ no registered hooks (empty list) -> []
+        ✔ flattens one level
+        ✔ filters out undefined
+        ✔ preserves null
+        ✔ all undefined -> []
+    hooks.callFirst
+      ✔ no registered hooks (undefined) -> []
+      ✔ no registered hooks (empty list) -> []
+      ✔ passes hook name => {}
+      ✔ undefined context => {}
+      ✔ null context => {}
+      ✔ context unmodified
+      ✔ predicate never satisfied -> calls all in order
+      ✔ stops when predicate is satisfied
+      ✔ skips values that do not satisfy predicate (undefined)
+      ✔ skips values that do not satisfy predicate (empty list)
+      ✔ null satisifes the predicate
+      ✔ non-empty arrays are returned unmodified
+      ✔ value can be passed via callback
+    callHookFnAsync
+      basic behavior
+        ✔ passes hook name
+        ✔ passes context
+        ✔ returns the value provided to the callback
+        ✔ returns the value returned by the hook function
+        ✔ rejects if it throws an exception
+        ✔ rejects if rejected Promise passed to callback
+        ✔ rejects if rejected Promise returned
+        ✔ callback returns undefined
+        ✔ checks for deprecation
+      supported hook function styles
+        ✔ return non-Promise value, with callback parameter
+        ✔ return non-Promise value, without callback parameter
+        ✔ return undefined, without callback parameter
+        ✔ pass non-Promise value to callback
+        ✔ pass undefined to callback
+        ✔ return the value returned from the callback
+        ✔ throw
+        ✔ return non-Promise value, with callback parameter
+        ✔ return non-Promise value, without callback parameter
+        ✔ return undefined, without callback parameter
+        ✔ pass non-Promise value to callback
+        ✔ pass undefined to callback
+        ✔ return the value returned from the callback
+        ✔ throw
+        ✔ legacy async cb

+        ✔ immediately call cb(value) then return value (diff. outcomes) -> log+throw
+        ✔ immediately call cb(value) then return value (same outcome) -> only log
+        ✔ immediately call cb(value) then immediately call cb(value) (diff. outcomes) -> log+throw
+        ✔ immediately call cb(value) then immediately call cb(value) (same outcome) -> only log

+        ✔ immediately call cb(value) then immediately call cb(unrejectedPromise) (diff. outcomes) -> log+throw
+        ✔ immediately call cb(value) then defer call to cb(value) (diff. outcomes) -> log+throw
+        ✔ immediately call cb(value) then defer call to cb(value) (same outcome) -> only log
+        ✔ immediately call cb(value) then defer call to cb(resolvedPromise) (diff. outcomes) -> log+throw
+        ✔ immediately call cb(value) then defer call to cb(resolvedPromise) (same outcome) -> only log
+        ✔ immediately call cb(value) then defer call to cb(rejectedPromise) (diff. outcomes) -> log+throw
+        ✔ immediately call cb(value) then defer call to cb(unresolvedPromise) (diff. outcomes) -> log+throw

+    hooks.aCallAll
+      basic behavior
+        ✔ calls all asynchronously, returns values in order
+        ✔ passes hook name
+        ✔ undefined context -> {}
+        ✔ null context -> {}
+        ✔ context unmodified
+      aCallAll callback
+        ✔ exception in callback rejects
+        ✔ propagates error on exception
+        ✔ propagages null error on success
+        ✔ propagages results on success
+        ✔ returns callback return value
+      result processing
+        ✔ no registered hooks (undefined) -> []
+        ✔ no registered hooks (empty list) -> []
+        ✔ flattens one level
+        ✔ filters out undefined
+        ✔ preserves null
+        ✔ all undefined -> []
+    hooks.callAllSerial
+      basic behavior
+        ✔ calls all asynchronously, serially, in order
+        ✔ passes hook name
+        ✔ undefined context -> {}
+        ✔ null context -> {}
+        ✔ context unmodified
+      result processing
+        ✔ no registered hooks (undefined) -> []
+        ✔ no registered hooks (empty list) -> []
+        ✔ flattens one level
+        ✔ filters out undefined
+        ✔ preserves null
+        ✔ all undefined -> []
+    hooks.aCallFirst
+      ✔ no registered hooks (undefined) -> []
+      ✔ no registered hooks (empty list) -> []
+      ✔ passes hook name => {}
+      ✔ undefined context => {}
+      ✔ null context => {}
+      ✔ context unmodified
+      ✔ default predicate: predicate never satisfied -> calls all in order
+      ✔ calls hook functions serially
+      ✔ default predicate: stops when satisfied
+      ✔ default predicate: skips values that do not satisfy (undefined)
+      ✔ default predicate: skips values that do not satisfy (empty list)
+      ✔ default predicate: null satisifes
+      ✔ custom predicate: called for each hook function
+      ✔ custom predicate: boolean false/true continues/stops iteration
+      ✔ custom predicate: non-boolean falsy/truthy continues/stops iteration
+      ✔ custom predicate: array value passed unmodified to predicate
+      ✔ custom predicate: normalized value passed to predicate (undefined)
+      ✔ custom predicate: normalized value passed to predicate (null)
+      ✔ non-empty arrays are returned unmodified
+      ✔ value can be passed via callback

+    hooks.aCallAll
+      basic behavior
+        ✔ calls all asynchronously, returns values in order
